In this way, both the predictions of models I and II, for a luminous mass in the range 4-6 10^ 11 M_ are in very good agreement with the preliminary new estimate from Shara Zurek (2002) of the nova rate for M87. In fact, M87 can be modeled both as an elliptical galaxy with luminous mass 4 10^ 11 M_ and R_ eff 6 kpc or with luminous mass 6 10^ 11 M_ and R_ eff 7 kpc (Cohen Ryzhov 1997). The predictions of these two models do not differ much and both of them can well represent M87.

sources.summary: The paper models M 87 as a giant elliptical radio galaxy whose TeV emission can probe cosmic-ray protons interacting with its interstellar medium.

quotes.quote: We examine the cosmic ray proton (CRp) population within the giant elliptical galaxy M 87 using the TeV ray detection of the HEGRA collaboration. In our scenario, the rays are produced by decaying pions which result from hadronic CRp interactions with thermal gas of the interstellar medium of M 87. By comparing the ray emission to upper limits from EGRET, we constrain the spectral index of the CRp population to _ GeV ^ TeV <2.275 within our scenario. Both the expected radial ray profile and the required amount of CRp support this hadronic scenario. The accompanying radio mini-halo of hadronically originating cosmic ray electrons is outshone by the synchrotron emission of the relativistic jet of M 87 by one order of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper includes M87 as a giant elliptical Virgo-cluster galaxy with a previously computed annihilation flux, and finds its flux above the Galactic foreground in the modeled cases.

quotes.quote: In our analysis we have also included the giant elliptical galaxy M87 at the center of the Virgo cluster whose predicted annihilation flux was already computed by . The mass, distance and the virial radius of M87, listed in Table tab1 , are taken from .

Filled triangles represent the fluxes from those LG galaxies which are brighter than the Galactic foreground and from M87, assuming a Moore profile. The fluxes from the Small and Large Magellanic Clouds (SMC, LMC), M31 and, to a lesser extent, M87 are above the Galactic level.
It turns out that, in all cases considered, the fluxes from the Small and the Large Magellanic Clouds, M31 and M87 are well above the level of the ray annihilation Galactic foreground.

sources.summary: The paper presents M87 as a nearby radio galaxy and weak TeV source whose jet, viewing angle, HEGRA detection, and non-blazar orientation make it notable.

quotes.quote: M87: This is one of the brightest nearby radio galaxies and is an obvious potential source of high energy radiation since the jet displays evidence for synchrotron radiation and time variability. The angle of the jet is about 30 ^ which means that it is unlikely to have the same observational gamma-ray properties as the blazars. In fact it was not detected by EGRET and the positive observation by the HEGRA group was a surprise. Although the detection was only at the 4 level of significance (weaker than any of the other sources in the TeV catalog) it is a potentially exciting result as it opens up the possibility that many AGN may be observable whose axes are not pointing directly towards us. It is a weak source and its detection required 83 hours of observation.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as an ionized-gas-disk prototype whose HST spectroscopy detected Keplerian nuclear rotation and yielded a central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: The second class consists of ionized gas without associated dust disks. M87 (Ford \ 1994) is the prototype of this category. Ionized disks are sometimes found to have filamentary or spiral structure, and patches of dust may be present as well.

The first target was M87, for which Harms \ (1994) detected a steep velocity gradient across the nucleus in the , [ N 2 ] , and [ O 3 ] emission lines, consistent with Keplerian rotation in an inclined disk. The central mass was found to be (2.4 0.7) 10^9 , remarkably close to the values first determined by Young \ (1978) and Sargent \ (1978).

sources.summary: M87 is described as a bright Virgo system whose X-ray spectra allow detailed plasma-composition measurements, including inner abundance values and element-to-iron dilution relative to stellar abundances.

quotes.quote: Because of its brightness, M87 in the Virgo cluster is among the most extensively X-ray spectroscopically studied extended extragalactic objects. Its proximity enables one to extract detailed plasma conditions and composition, and to evaluate and correct for systematic effects. Previous work based on spectra from the Einstein (Canizares et al. 1982; Stewart et al. 1984; White et al. 1991; Tsai 1994a, b), ROSAT (Nulsen Bohringer 1995; Buote 2001), and ASCA (Matsumoto et al. 1996; Hwang et al. 1997; Buote, Canizares, Fabian 1999; Finoguenov Jones 2000; Shibata et al. 2001) observatories is rapidly becoming superseded by Chandra and XMM-Newton data. The RGS results for M87 show that, in the inner 10 kpc, abundances of C, N, Ne, and Mg have best-fit values (relative to Anders Grevesse 1989) of 0.7--1.0 solar, with 90 uncertainties of 0.2--0.3, while Fe is 0.77 0.04 solar, and O 0.49 0.04 solar (Sakelliou et al. 2002). With respect to the M87 stellar abundances (Milone, Barbuy, Schiavon 2000), the elements are apparently preferentially diluted with respect to Fe.

sources.summary: The paper studies M87 with Chandra and HST, characterizing its LMXB population, globular-cluster connections, and basic distance and structural assumptions.

quotes.quote: M87 (NGC 4486), the giant elliptical galaxy near the dynamical center of the Virgo Cluster, has the richest GC system in the local supercluster with a total of 13,450 950 GCs (McLaughlin, Harris Hanes 1994). It is also one of the most thoroughly studied GC systems, with a wealth of spectroscopic and photometric information available on its metallicity distribution, spatial structure, luminosity function, age distribution, and dynamical properties (McLaughlin, Harris Hanes 1994; Cohen, Blakeslee Ryzhov 1998; Harris et al. 1998; Kundu et al. 1999; Hanes et al. 2001; C ot e et al. 2001; Kissler-Patig, Brodie Minniti 2002; Jord an et al. 2002). Yet nothing is known about the LMXB population in M87 or its relation to the M87 GC system, partly due to the difficulty of detecting individual X-ray point sources superimposed on a bright, and spatially varying diffuse background. In this paper, we combine deep X-ray observations from Chandra with optical g_ 475 and z_ 850 imaging from HST to characterize the LMXB population in M87, and to examine its connection to the underlying GC system. In what follows, we assume a distance to M87 of D = 16 Mpc (Tonry et al. 2001), an effective radius of R_e = 96'' (de Vaucouleurs Nieto 1978), and a Galactic column density of N_H=2.5 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (Stark et al. 1992).

sources.summary: As a comparison jet, M87 is described as similarly well studied, with radio-to-X-ray SED curvature requiring a second spectral component beyond simple synchrotron cutoff models.

quotes.quote: It is notable that besides 3C 273, the spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of M87's jet shows the same upward curvature: Mar02 have presented SEDs for that jet from radio through X-rays (their Fig. 3). They fitted two different single-population models, a Jaffe-Perola model assuming a randomly tangled magnetic field structure and pitch-angle scattering, and a Kardashev-Pacholczyk model, also assuming a tangled field but no pitch angle scattering. Only the latter provides an acceptable fit to the observed SEDs, but it is unclear how pitch-angle scattering could be avoided in a tangled field geometry. Moreover, the spectra for knots HST-1 and D clearly show a steeper downturn near 10^ 15 Hz already hinted at by the ground-based spectra obtained by their Figs. 7 and 8 MRS96 than the Kardashev-Pacholczyk spectrum, which is therefore inadequate. Instead, again a second spectral component must be invoked to account for the X-ray flux lying above the cutoff observed in the near-infrared--optical region.

sources.summary: The paper presents a 3 mm VLBI map of M 87, emphasizing its resolvable central engine, radio-loud jet, and suitability for event-horizon-scale imaging.

quotes.quote: At a distance of 18.7 Mpc of M 87, the angular resolution of 300 x 60 as corresponds to a spatial scale of 30 x 6 light days, or 100 x 20 Schwarzschild radii (assuming a 3 x 10^9 M _ BH). Thus, the central engine and the inner jet can be studied with a similar spatial resolution as the less massive, but closer SMBH in Sgr A*. In fact, owing to its higher declination, M 87 is easier to observe with VLBI and might be even a better candidate than Sgr A* for the imaging of the event horizon around a SMBH.
One of the main differences between these two objects of course is, that the jet of M 87 is related to a radio-loud galaxy, whereas Sgr A* has a much lower radio-luminosity and shows no jet.
The fact that in M 87 the jet can be traced down to scales of only a few ten Schwarzschild radii without a large reduction of its brightness temperature is very noteworthy.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST observations of M 87 as the fiducial low-luminosity radio galaxy, constraining its compact emission-line region to under 3 pc and finding nuclear gas densities rising to about 4300 cm^-3.

quotes.quote: In the following we focus on 3C 274 (M 87), the closer and best studied object of the sample. First of all, the size of the compact emitting region in M 87 is constrained by the HST images to be smaller than R_ CELR < 0 05 3 pc as this is unresolved at 0 1 resolution. The electrons density in its central emission line region has been derived by macchetto:m87 from HST/FOS spectra measuring the ratio of the sulfur lines [ S II ] 6731 / [ S II ] 6717 . Moving toward the nucleus this ratio decreases reaching 0.6 0.1 at the central aperture, still far from the ratio corresponding to the high density limit (0.42). This is indicative of an increase of density from a few hundreds to about 4300 cm ^ 3 (with a factor of 2 accuracy) at the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper uses intracluster planetary nebula velocities to show that M87 has an extended stellar halo in approximate dynamical equilibrium out to at least 65 kpc.

quotes.quote: The M87 peak of the FCJ velocity distribution contains 12 velocities with v _ p = 1276 71 km s ^ 1 and _ p = 247 52 km s ^ 1 . The average velocity is consistent with that of M87, v_ sys = 1258 km s ^ 1 . The distance of the center of the FCJ field from the center of M87 is 15.'0 65 kpc for an assumed M87 distance of 15 Mpc. The value of _p is very consistent with the stellar velocity dispersion profile extrapolated outwards from 150'' in Figure 5 of Romanowsky Kochanek (2001) and falls in the range spanned by their dynamical models for the M87 stars. The main result from our measurement of _p is that M87 has a stellar halo in approximate dynamical equilibrium out to at least 65 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper reports strong 15 GHz VLBA core circular polarization in M87, contrasts it with VLA-scale measurements, and attributes differences to scale, time, or frequency effects.

quotes.quote: 1228 + 126 (M87): Figure f:m87ipv.ps shows our 15 GHz VLBA image of the jet of M87 in February of 2003. No linear polarization is detected, but strong circular polarization, m_c = -0.49 0.10 is seen at the peak of the core. M87 was studied at the VLA in April of 2000 by BFM02 , who found no significant circular polarization at 8 GHz, m_c = 0.01 0.10 ; however, they did find linear polarization at the 1.7 level. These results are the opposite of our finding of strong circular polarization and no linear polarization. It is important to consider that their VLA results include contributions from the jet well beyond the region imaged by the VLBA, and the linear polarization they observe must come from beyond the depolarizing screen which covers most of the VLBA scale jet ZT02 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 for parameter tests, finds boundary-radius changes raise X-ray luminosity by over an order, and describes it as an elliptical radio galaxy with jets or lobes.

quotes.quote: For NGC4472, M87 and IC1459 the difference between L_X and L_ X,modeled ranges from 3-4 orders of magnitude.
Sources where L_X/L_ X,modeled is between 3 and 4 orders of magnitude namely NGC4472 and M87 are ellipticals radio galaxies with jets or radio lobes.

In Fig: Fig:11 we also show spectra of M87 for different boundary radius equal 10^6 R_ in . This change affect only the bremsstrahlung part of the spectrum, which is created by the photons emitted from the outer shells of the spherical cloud. log_ 10 (L_X) increase to log_ 10 (L_X=38.24) , more than one order of magnitude.

sources.summary: Virgo A is treated as the comparison XMM target whose earlier sterile-neutrino limit, dark-matter mass estimate, and higher X-ray background are contrasted with Andromeda.

quotes.quote: Based on XMM-Newton observations of Virgo A (M87), the dominant galaxy in the northern part of the Virgo cluster , Abazajian arrived at an improved upper limit of m_s < 8.2 keV (95 C.L.). Boyarsky et al. have also used XMM-Newton observations of Virgo A and the Coma cluster to explore possible constraints on m_s , but they do not provide a definite numerical limit.
B ohringer et al. used XMM to measure the flux of and around Virgo A (M87) out to a radius of 12 ' ( 55 kpc at D_ M87 15.8 0.8 Mpc ) over a (usable) exposure time of 25.9 ks.

In Ref. , AFT integrate an isothermal model to estimate the dark matter mass of Virgo A within a 17 ' 17 ' rectangular prism. When we integrate the same model over a truncated cone of cross-sectional radius _ fov =8.5' and length 2R_ vir 3.6 Mpc , we find a mass of M^ fov _ DM,M87 (0.75 0.08) 10^ 13 M_ , which agrees with the result of AFT ( M^ fov _ DM,M87 10^ 13 M_ ) to within a factor of V_ fov, cone /V_ fov, prism /4 .

sources.summary: The paper studies M 87 as a giant elliptical with a hot ISM, measuring its temperature structure and abundances to infer enrichment by supernovae and asymptotic giant branch stars.

quotes.quote: We present here a study of chemical abundances in the giant elliptical galaxy M 87 using high-resolution spectra obtained with the Reflection Grating Spectrometers during two deep XMM-Newton observations. While we confirm the two-temperature structure of the inter-stellar medium (ISM) in M 87, we also show that a continuous temperature distribution describes the data equally well. The high statistics allows us, for the first time, to determine relatively accurate abundance values also for carbon and nitrogen.

The comparison of the abundance ratios of C, N, O and Fe in the ISM of M 87 with those in the stellar population of our Galaxy shows that the relative contribution of core-collapse supernovae to the enrichment of the ISM in M 87 is significantly less than in the Milky Way and indicates that the enrichment of the ISM by iron through Type Ia supernovae and by carbon and nitrogen is occurring in parallel. This suggests that the main source of carbon and nitrogen in M 87 are the low- and intermediate-mass asymptotic giant branch stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes M87 as Virgo's dominant central galaxy, used to study ICM enrichment, with a supermassive black hole, one-sided jet, radio lobes, and clear AGN/ICM interaction.

quotes.quote: M87 Galaxy M87 is the dominant central galaxy in the Virgo Cluster. Because M87 is very close and bright it was used to study the role of SNIa and SNII in enriching the ICM (e.g. Gastaldello Molendi 2002, Matsushita et al. 2003, Finoguenov et al. 2002). There is clear dynamical evidence of the presence of a supermassive black hole in its core (e.g. Macchetto et al. 1997), with a one-sided jet (e.g. Schreier et al.1982, Owen et al. 1989, Sparks et al. 1996). At least two gas components with different temperatures are observed: the hotter component is almost symmetric around the central galaxy (Ma tsushita et al. 2002), while the cooler component forms extended structures correlated with the radio lobes. There are clear signs of an AGN/ICM interaction in this source both through the shocks and sound waves (Forman et al. 2005, 2006) and through mechanical entrainment of the cool gas by the bubbles of relativistic plasma (Churazov et al. 2001).

sources.summary: The paper uses M 87 as the principal comparison object for Centaurus, contrasting ICM abundance patterns, Mg gradients, Fe abundance, and SN Ia accumulation timescales.

quotes.quote: In this section, we mainly compare and discuss the abundance pattern of the Centaurus clusters with that of M 87, since these two clusters are the nearest and the best studied objects among relatively low temperature clusters. The gradient of the Mg abundance of the gas around M 87 is steeper than in the Centaurus cluster. At a radius of 10--20 kpc, the Mg abundance of the Centaurus cluster is a factor of 2 larger than that of M 87 (Figure mgo ).

The Fe abundance of the ICM of the Centaurus cluster is systematically higher than that around M 87 at a same distance from the cluster center (Figure mgas0 ). Since the contribution of the SN Ia to Fe in the ICM is dominant, this indicates that accumulation time scale of SN Ia is higher in the Centaurus cluster than that in M 87, although we do not know the actual gas flow rate due to the cooling flow problem.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87's repeated weak shocks to argue that modest shock heating near the AGN can plausibly offset radiative cooling in the surrounding gas.

quotes.quote: Second, the properties of the repeated weak shocks in M87 are used to show that they can plausibly prevent gas close to the AGN from cooling. As the most significant heating mechanism at work closest to the AGN, shock heating probably plays a critical role in the feedback mechanism.

Three weak shocks are visible in the X-ray image of M87 M87 . Fitting a simple model to the surface brightness profile of the innermost shock at 0.8 arcmin ( 3.7 kpc) gives a Mach number of 1.4 and a shock age of 2.4 10^6 y.
These numbers are not to be taken literally, but they make a plausible case that the modest heating of repeated weak shocks can stop the gas at 0.8 arcmin from the AGN in M87 from cooling.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as evidence for very massive black holes and nearby jet launching, noting its jet reaches within about 100 gravitational radii of the central black hole.

quotes.quote: The existence of such massive black holes in some galactic nuclei is confirmed by the evaluation of the black mass in M87 .
The presence of such a flow in M87 was proposed by Reynolds et al. rey96 .

Independent argument for the formation of extra galactic jets in the vicinity of supermassive black holes is provided by direct VLBI observations of nearby radio galaxies. In particular, in 3C 274 (M87) the jet is seen down to 10^ 16 cm from the center , which corresponds to 100 gravitational radii for the 3 10^9 M_ central black hole. There, very deep in the gravitational potential well, jets could be powered either by the innermost parts of an accretion disc (Blandford Payne 1982; Park Vishniac 1994; Contopoulos 1995; Begelman 1995) or by a rotating black hole (Blandford Znajek 1977; Rees et al. 1982).

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a TeV AGN whose HST1 knot may explain rapid X-ray/TeV variability, with low TeV luminosity, estimated jet power, and stringent opening-angle requirements.

quotes.quote: Such a model has been proposed to explain the rapid variability of the resolved X-ray emission and the unresolved TeV emission from the HST1 knot in M87 .

It should be noted though that in M87 the X-ray and TeV luminosities, L_ TeV L_ x 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , are much smaller than the TeV luminosity, L_ TeV 10^ 44-45 erg s ^ 1 , observed typically in the TeV blazars. Estimates of the jet power in M87 yield L_j 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , implying a very small conversion fraction, L_ TeV /L_j 10^ 3 . Even with such a small conversion efficiency an opening angle <10^ 2 rad is required if the TeV emission were to originate from the HST1 knot, unless reconfinement can give rise to sufficient convergence of the jet at the location of HST1, as proposed in Ref .

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a nearby giant elliptical AGN whose jet may accelerate cosmic-ray protons, applying the transport model to M87-like injection and discussing its possible ultra-high-energy cosmic-ray output.

quotes.quote: One of these nearby AGN is the giant elliptical galaxy M87. It is proposed that this galaxy is responsible for acceleration of cosmic ray protons due to Fermi I processes Bland,Rieger in shockfronts within the jet Reimer .
The first test case where we applied our model to is a jet-like injection shape like in M87.

As a result of the basic possibility of explaining this power law-with physical parameters that might be found in M87, our calculation gives rise to the theory of M87 as a source of ultra-high energy cosmic rays.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 87 as the benchmark for radiatively inefficient accretion, noting its very low-radiative-efficiency central black hole and comparing AEGIS galaxies' higher Eddington ratios to it.

quotes.quote: We explore this possibility by comparing the mean Eddington ratio of 24 m bright galaxies with C>-0.15 with that of M 87, the best studied example of a system undergoing radiatively inefficient accretion. The central supermassive BH of this galaxy has been shown to have very low radiative efficiency, corresponding to an Eddington ratio of 10^ 7 (Di Matteo et al. 2003).

The BH mass and the L_ bol estimates above translate to a lower limit for the Eddington ratio of 5 10^ 4 , more than 3 dex higher than the corresponding quantity for M 87. If the hard X-ray signal of 24 m bright galaxies with C>-0.15 is associated with a radiatively inefficient accretion mode, they have to be significantly less extreme than M 87.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as an FR-I observational comparison, citing limb-brightened parsec jets, superluminal motion on arcsecond scales, and agreement with low-inclination synthetic maps.

quotes.quote: Moreover, VLBI observations of some object like B2 1144+35, Mrk501 and M87 show limb brightening of the jet radio emission at the parsec scale. This effect is interpreted kinematically as due to the presence of a deboosted central spine at high Lorentz factor and of a weakly relativistic external layer.

Superluminal motions are observed on milliarcsecond scales in several FR-I jets Giovannini01 and on arcsecond scales in M87 and Cen A Biretta95, Hardcastle03 and finally, on small scales, there are also observations of one-sidedness and brightness asymmetry between jet and counter-jet, due most likely to Doppler boosting effects Laing99 .
The VLBA map of M87 can be compared with our synthetic map at low inclination angle, which agrees with observational estimates.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as a giant FRI radio galaxy and LLAGN with a relativistic jet, a massive black hole, low bolometric luminosity, and jet-dominated radio emission.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of the giant FRI radio-galaxy M87, which displays a radio, optical, and X-ray relativistic jet at all scales from 100 R_ G up to kiloparsecs, contains a 3 10 ^9 M _ black hole. It has a bolometric luminosity much fainter than the Eddington luminosity, and at least two orders of magnitude smaller than the Bondi accretion rate from the hot gas surrounding the BH It is the accretion rate of a spherical flow with a radial velocity equal to the sound velocity. (Fabian Rees , Di Matteo et al. ). M87 is a LLAGN, as are all FRI galaxies, as well as radio-quiet LINERs.
This is clear when the LLAGN is a FRI radio-galaxy like M87, as the jet is prominent.

sources.summary: M 87 is treated as a benchmark FRI radio galaxy whose jet SED, knot variability, and optical/radio polarization constrain synchrotron emission and shock acceleration.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:sed:m87-0637 compares the spectral distribution of the FRI radio galaxy M 87, where a broken-power-law synchrotron components fits well, with that of the FRII quasar PKS 0637-752.

The knots of Cen A are not highly variable in observations to date , but dramatic variability on a timescale of months is seen in a knot in the jet of M 87, and the X-ray, optical and radio light curves are broadly consistent with shock acceleration, expansion, and energy losses, although the timeline is currently too short for strong conclusions to be drawn .

The first jet to be studied in detail in both its radio and optical polarized emission was M 87, where there is evidence for strong shock acceleration in compressed transverse magnetic fields at the base of bright emitting regions, although the polarization fraction becomes low at the flux maxima . Significant differences between the polarization structures seen in the optical and radio suggest that the sites of acceleration are different for different electron energies, with the strongest shocks, that provide acceleration to the highest energies, appearing in the most central parts of the jet .

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a photon-rich AGN environment where primary TeV photons are unlikely to escape, while cosmic rays could survive and generate observed secondary photons.

quotes.quote: For example, the intrinsic photon densities around M87 and 3C279 can be inferred from observations, and these densities appear inconsistent with the unattenuated emission of VHE photons 2008AIPC.1085..644C,2008ApJ...685L..23A .

The photon-rich environments of M87 and 3C279 are not likely to allow the primary TeV photons to come out  2008AIPC.1085..644C . However, the cosmic rays are not attenuated significantly by the same backgrounds. The observations of M87 and 3C279 are consistent with the secondary photon interpretation.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a nearby FRI active galaxy and possible cosmic-ray accelerator, scaling its TeV flux to compare with Cen A and motivate a generic FRI neutrino model.

quotes.quote: If AGN are indeed the sources, the proximity of the Fanaroff-Riley I (FRI) active galaxies Cen A and M87 singles them out as potential accelerators .

We scaled the flux of M87 at 16 Mpc to the distance to Cen A. After adjusting for the different thresholds of the HEGRA and Sydney experiments, we find identical source luminosities for M87 and Cen A of roughly 7 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 , assuming an E^ 2 gamma-ray spectrum. The time-averaged gamma-ray flux thus obtained is close to the flux from Cen A recently observed by the H.E.S.S. collaboration . Given that we obtain identical luminosities for Cen A and M87, we venture the assumption that they may be generic FRI, a fact we will exploit to construct the diffuse neutrino flux from all FRI.

sources.summary: The paper links high-surface-brightness Virgo planetary-nebula correlations to M87's halo, arguing that many candidates in Field 3/FCJ are likely associated with M87 rather than true intracluster light.

quotes.quote: On these scales, we find a correlation in regions of higher surface brightness ( 27) which are dominated by the halos of large galaxies such as M87, M86, and M84. In those cases, we are likely tracing PNe associated with galaxies rather than true IPNe.

For example Field 3/FCJ, which is dominated by halo light from M87, shows a clear gradient in PNe density from the NE to the SW, along the direction of M87's luminosity gradient. Many of these PNe are likely associated with M87 itself rather than being true intracluster PNe (see, the discussion in F04); indeed, followup spectroscopy of 15 IPN candidates in the FCJ field by Arnaboldi (2004) shows that 12 of the 15 candidates have a narrow velocity distribution ( =247 52 km/s) that can be associated with M87's halo.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as an AGN jet-disk test case: its nucleus rotates clockwise, its jet spine has negative Faraday rotation, and the PR battery model matches these signs.

quotes.quote: For two objects in which the sense of rotation of the underlying accretion disk is known, our model is in agreement with the observed Faraday-rotation signs if these jets are viewed at angles of less than 1/ . HST direct imaging of the M87 nucleus (Tsevtanov et al. 1999) and maser emission in the inner disk of NGC 4258 (Hernstein et al. 2007) reveal that the accretion disks in these systems rotate CW on the sky. FR measurements along the jet spine in M87 (Zavala Taylor 2002) and in NGC 4258 (Krause Lohr 2004) yield large negative FR values ( B_ LOS directed away from the observer). Thus, in the PR battery model, an observer viewing the jet at an angle of less than 1/ would be located in the S hemisphere (Fig. 1b), and these two accretion disks should indeed be rotating CW on the sky.

sources.summary: The paper describes a 2008 joint Cherenkov campaign on the radio galaxy M 87, including a MAGIC-triggered high-emission episode tied to radio and VHE variability near the central black hole.

quotes.quote: M 87 is a nearby Radio Galaxy located in the center of the Virgo cluster and was the first Radio Galaxy to be detected at VHE rays . In 2008 a joint campaign involving all three next generation Cherenkov telescopes (H.E.S.S., MAGIC and VERITAS) observed M 87 for in total 120h from January until May. In February, MAGIC detected a strongly increased emission from M 87 and triggered MWL observations with radio, X-ray and extended VHE coverage . The data show evidence for a correlated radio and VHE ray emission and allowed to constrain the origin of the VHE ray emission to the immediate vincinity of the central black hole.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as an extremely misaligned radio source whose counterjet-side hot spot and radio luminosity make it a possible hybrid radio morphology object.

quotes.quote: One interesting source that shows extreme misalignment ( 135 ) is M87 (1228+126). M87 appears to exhibit a weak hot spot on the counterjet side e.g., Owen90,Sparks92 . Since the brightest emission on the counterjet side is at the extremity of the radio lobe (like in FRIIs), M87 could be classified as a hybrid radio morphology source. Its radio luminosity ( L_ 178 =1 10^ 25 W m ^ 2 ) also places it close to the FRI/FRII division Biretta99 .

sources.summary: NGC 4486 is described as M 87/Virgo A, Virgo's dominant central galaxy, hosting radio-bright 3C 274, a bright radio and X-ray jet, and complex large-scale ICM/radio structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486 NGC 4486, also known as M 87 (Virgo A), is the dominant galaxy at the centre of the Virgo Cluster. It hosts the well studied radio-bright AGN 3C 274. A bright radio jet is also clearly visible in the VLA images, although the contours plotted have been chosen to highlight the diffuse emission surrounding the core. The jet is also clearly seen at X-ray wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a high-specific-frequency comparison galaxy, noting its large globular cluster population and an SMBH mass above the mass-dispersion expectation.

quotes.quote: The dominant galaxy M87 has an even larger specific frequency rac68 , estimated by Peng et al. \ to be S_N 13 .

Compare the galaxies M87 (NGC 4486) and Fornax A (NGC 1316), which have similar luminosities ( M_V=-22.7 and 22.8 respectively). M87 has an SMBH mass that is larger than expected for its dispersion by 0.20 dex, while Fornax A contains an unusually small SMBH, by 0.42 dex. M87 has a specific frequency S_N = 12.2 that is a factor of 3 larger than the average value for our sample of S_N = 4.0 , while Fornax A is characterized by an unusually small value, S_N = 0.9 .

sources.summary: The paper presents M87/Virgo as an AGN-feedback example with cool gas arcs linked to radio jets and lobes, a weak spherical central shock, and sound-wave ripples.

quotes.quote: There is a clear association with arcs of cool gas and the radio jets and lobes in the M87/Virgo system .
A deep Chandra image of M87/Virgo revealed an approximately spherical shock in the cluster center . The shock is most clearly seen in a hard-band image, and is fairly weak with a Mach number of 1.2.
In addition to the Perseus cluster, sound wave ripples are seen in the Centaurus cluster and M87/Virgo .

sources.summary: The paper describes M 87/NGC 4486 as Virgo’s brightest radio galaxy and dynamic center, offset from the centroid of the cluster’s blueshifted galaxies.

quotes.quote: The bulk of the population of Virgo is concentrated around the brightest galaxy NGC 4486 (M 87). The X-ray intensity peak of the hot intergalactic gas in Virgo also lies in M 87, which suggests that this radio galaxy is the dynamic center of the cluster.
The position of the galaxy M 87 as the physical center of the cluster is indicated by a cross.
(b) The centroid of the present sample does not coincide with the dynamic center of Virgo (M 87), and is shifted significantly to the NW by 1^ . 10 0^ . 35 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a jet-system example, giving its black-hole mass, accretion rate, luminosity, inferred jet-base magnetic field, and fast collisionless reconnection regime.

quotes.quote: First, consider the case of the jet in M87 (similar arguments apply to Blazars), which has a black hole with mass M 6 10^9 accreting at M 10^ 2 / yr with a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 10^ 42 s ^ 1 gt09 , and so operates at a radiative efficiency of order 0.01 corresponding to a low-luminosity radiatively inefficient accretion flow. Equipartition arguments combined with GRMHD simulations of accretion disks and the Blandford-Znajek funnel region mckinney2004 then imply that the magnetic field strength at the base of the jet is of order B_ , fp 10^3 G. Our solutions show that the M87 jet is already in the fast collisionless regime at the base of the jet (see also gub10 ). This suggests that an electromagnetically-dominated jet from M87 would not survive unless an ordered dipolar field is present at the jet base mb09 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses M87 as a nearby nonblazar AGN whose modest jet can strip red-giant material, forming clouds that may explain detectable gamma-ray emission despite de-boosting.

quotes.quote: Originally, the scenario of AGN jet -- red giant (RG) interaction has been suggested by bab10 for the explanation of VHE observations of M87 - a nonblazar type nearby AGN with a large jet viewing angle of 20^ and a modest jet power, of L_ jet 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 . It was demonstrated that if disturbed by tidal forces RG penetrates the jet, the ram pressure of the jet in M87 would be sufficient to remove the outer layer of the RG. This leads to the formation of a dense cloud within the jet which, in combination with effective particle acceleration, can trigger gamma-ray production through proton-proton interactions which passes secure of the required spectral and temporal properties. This model allows detectable gamma-ray fluxes because of the proximity of the source and the non-relativistic speed of the blobs (otherwise the radiation from M87 would be de-boosted given the large aspect angle of the jet).

sources.summary: The paper describes M87's FIRST morphology as similar to radio-jet and cocoon images, emphasizing its SMBH-intracluster-medium interaction and X-ray rings driven by outbursts.

quotes.quote: The FIRST image of M87, a three-component group, Figure M87 , is similar in morphology to the 6 cm VLA radio image from Hines89 showing the radio jet and synchrotron emission from the cocoon. They suggested M87 provides a unique laboratory for investigating the interaction of a SMBH and surrounding intracluster medium. Forman07 in a study of M87 reported a nearly circular ring of outer radius 2.8' at hard energies (3.5-7.5 keV) and at 0.6' and 1' (the approximate radius of the FIRST ring), surface brightness edges and pressure enhancements. Forman07 suggest this cocoon is the "piston" that mediates outbursts from the central MSBH and drives shocks into the surrounding X-ray-emitting, thermal gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes M87 as the nearest TeV-emitting cD radio galaxy, using its jet, halo, and multiwavelength spectral energy distribution to test WIMP dark-matter annihilation models.

quotes.quote: The first radio galaxy detected in the TeV energy range, M87 is one of the best studied in its source class with a jet resolved by optical, radio and X-ray observations. This cD galaxy is the nearest radio galaxy emitting very high energy (VHE) gamma rays and is known for its proximity to the Earth ( D = 16 Mpc ) and exceptionally bright arcsecond scale jet . M87 is an excellent laboratory in which to study dark matter because of its location in the central high mass density region of the Virgo cluster. In this work, the imprint of WIMP annihilation radiation from the halo structure of M87 in the form of prompt and inverse-Compton gamma rays is investigated, and dark matter particles are constrained, via a multiwavelength analysis of the spectral energy distribution of M87.

sources.summary: The paper models M87's flat TeV emission with a general-relativistic external inverse Compton scenario using ADAF soft photons and mini-jet electrons, motivated by its TeV variability and blazar-like nuclear SED.

quotes.quote: M87 is the first detected non-blazar extragalactic Tera-Electron-Volt (TeV) source with rapid variation and very flat spectrum in the TeV band. To explain the two-peaks in the spectral energy distribution (SED) of the nucleus of M87 which is similar to those of blazars, the most commonly adopted models are the synchrotron self-Compton scattering (SSC) models and the external inverse Compton scattering (EIC) models. Considering that there is no correlated variation in the soft band (from radio to X-ray) matching the TeV variation, and the TeV sources should not suffer from the absorption due to the flat TeV spectrum, the EIC models are advantageous in modeling the TeV emission from M87. In this paper, we propose a self-consistent EIC model to explain the flat TeV spectrum of M87 within the framework of fully general relativity, where the background soft photons are from the advection-dominated accretion flow (ADAF) around the central black hole, and the high energy electrons are from the mini-jets which are powered by the magnetic reconnection in the main jet 2010MNRAS.402.1649G .

sources.summary: The paper uses prior Chandra, SED, and HST jet constraints for NGC 4486/M87, adopting jet orientation parameters and requiring the model jet power to match observed estimates.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486 dmt03 previously calculated the Bondi accretion rate using Chandra X-ray data and found it to be M _ Bondi 0.1 \ M_ \ yr ^ 1 ( m _ Bondi 7 10^ 4 ). They also fitted the multiwavelength SED of M87 with an ADAF model for different values of (but not including mass-loss in the accretion flow, i.e. s=0 ) and found that the ADAF emission roughly explains the SED. biretta99 analysed HST observations of the jet in M87 and estimated that _j 6 and 10^ <i<19^ . Based on the results of biretta99 , we adopt in our jet modelling the parameters _j=6 and i=10^ .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4486 has less secure optical/NIR globular-cluster bimodality than NGC 4649; (g-K) bimodality appears only in a bright, low-error subset, and scatter may erase it.

quotes.quote: The two most cluster-rich galaxies in our sample, NGC 4486 and NGC 4649, show some interesting differences. The (g-K) distribution of NGC 4649 is better described by a bimodal distribution, while this is true for the (g-K) distribution of NGC 4486 GCs only if restricted to a brighter sub-sample with small K-band errors ( < 0.05 mag).

However, simulations including the extra scatter in the colour-colour diagrams (not fully accounted for in the photometric errors) show that such scatter may contribute to the disappearance of bimodality in (g-K) for the full NGC 4486 sample.
However, for NGC 4486 the situation is less clear and bimodality is detected at a statistically significant level only for the brightest sub-sample of the clusters in (g-K) .

sources.summary: The paper models M87's April 2010 TeV flare as emission from a massive cloud or red giant interaction with the jet near the central black hole.

quotes.quote: M87, a nearby representative of this class, shows fast TeV variability on timescales less than one day. We present calculations performed in the framework of the scenario in which gamma-ray flares in non-blazar active galactic nuclei are produced by a red giant or a gas cloud interacting with the jet. We show that both the light curve and energy spectrum of the spectacular April 2010 flare can be reproduced by this model, assuming that a relatively massive cloud of 10^ 29  g penetrates into the jet at few tens of Schwarzschild radii from the super-massive black hole.

Because of its proximity ( 16.7 0.2 Mpc; mbc07 ) and the very massive black hole at its center, with mass M_ BH (3 - 6) 10^ 9 M _ mma97,gt09 , high resolution very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) at radio wavelengths enables one to directly probe structures with sizes down to <100 Schwarzschild radii ( R_ Sch ).

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as the Virgo-cluster BCG with a resolved Bondi radius, no radiatively efficient inner disk, and a jet traced close to the black hole.

quotes.quote: Good observations of the inner regions of the flow onto a massive black hole in a BCG are of M87 at the centre of the Virgo cluster. The Bondi radius there is 100--200 pc, corresponding to 1--2 arcsec (Di Matteo et al 2003). There is no evidence for a radiatively-efficient accretion disc extending in to the black hole. The nearside jet is well seen and resolved down to very close to the black hole (it is accelerated within 100 r_ g ; Hada et al 2011).

sources.summary: The review describes M87/Virgo A as a Virgo-cluster radio galaxy with inefficient SMBH accretion, multi-scale emission, and jet power exceeding its radiative output.

quotes.quote: Perhaps the best example of such a powerful ``low efficiency'' black hole is the radio galaxy Virgo A, which is the product of inefficient accretion onto the supermassive black hole at the center of M87, which itself is located in the center of the Virgo cluster.

fig:cavities ) Similarly, the privileged view we enjoy of the nearby radio galaxy Virgo A (also known as M87) allowed a uniquely detailed study of its multi-scale emission well before the idea of feedback had taken hold.

Anecdotally, the case of the M87 jet makes the perfect illustration of this point: The total radiative output from the black hole is unimpressive Much of this radiation may actually be in the form of X-rays from the unresolved base of the jets itself at L_ M87 10^ 41 ergs s^ 1 , about two orders of magnitude below the luminosity corresponding to efficient accretion at the Bondi rate of that particular black hole. Meanwhile, the estimated jet power from this object is about two orders of magnitude larger forman:07 , consistent with the accretion power one would derive if the black hole were accreting at the Bondi accretion rate, thus making M87 a jet-dominated low-luminosity black hole.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87's observed radio-jet geometry to define a parabolic-to-conical jet model, treating its observations as fixed geometry parameters while testing whether that structure can fit a blazar.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2011 In this paper we develop the jet model of Potter Cotter (2012) to include a magnetically dominated accelerating parabolic base transitioning to a slowly decelerating conical jet with a geometry set by recent radio observations of M87.

We fit the geometry of our model to the observed geometry of M87 ( ), though we leave the opening angle of the conical section of the jet as a free parameter since this is likely to vary with the bulk Lorentz factor of the jet as 1 _ bulk .

This means that the entire geometry of our jet is described by only two free parameters (the others are set by the observations of M87) the black hole mass M and the lab frame conical half-opening angle _ opening . We do not expect the geometry of the inner region of all AGN jets to be the same as that of M87, however, it is very interesting to see whether a jet with this geometry is compatible with the observations of a blazar.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4486 has weak or no rotation, the largest aperture-dependent dispersion variation, and a lower luminosity-weighted dispersion after excluding the black-hole sphere of influence.

quotes.quote: The amplitude of the projected rotation velocity ranges from 40 km s ^ 1 to over 200 km s ^ 1 while six galaxies, namely NGC 1068, NGC 3608, NGC 4261, NGC 4374, NGC 4486 and NGC 6251 show a weak or no rotation component.

For example, NGC 4486 shows the largest variation in as a function of aperture size since it has no significant rotation component while the velocity dispersion profile is strongly decreasing toward larger radii.

We performed a similar analysis using our data although only two galaxies, NGC 4486 and NGC 4649, are among those 12 galaxies with a resolved sphere of influence. By excluding the sphere of influence of BH, the luminosity-weighted decreases from 346 12 to 327 11 for NGC 4486, and from 346 12 to 327 11 for NGC 4649.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 jet observations as the geometric template for its blazar jet model, emphasizing the parabolic-to-conical transition at about 10^5 Schwarzschild radii.

quotes.quote: We include an accelerating parabolic base transitioning to a slowly decelerating conical jet with a geometry set by observations of M87 and consistent with simulations and theory. We investigate several jet models and find that the optically thick to thin synchrotron break in the radio spectrum requires the jet to first come into equipartition at large distances along the jet, consistent with the observed transition from parabolic to conical at 10^ 5 R_ s in the jet of M87.

We set the geometry of our model using the observations of the geometry of M87 with lengths scaled linearly with black hole mass. If we assume a jet geometry similar to that observed in M87 we expect the jet to transition from parabolic to conical at 10^ 5 R_ s .

sources.summary: The paper models M87 AGN feedback, using LOFAR radio-halo constraints and gamma-ray emission to argue cosmic rays from radio lobes mix with intracluster gas and can balance radiative cooling.

quotes.quote: New observations of M87 enable us to put forward a comprehensive model for the physical heating mechanism. Low-frequency radio observations by LOFAR revealed the absence of fossil cosmic ray (CR) electrons in the radio halo surrounding M87. This puzzle can be resolved by accounting for the CR release from the radio lobes and the subsequent mixing of CRs with the dense ambient intracluster gas, which thermalizes the electrons on a timescale similar to the radio halo age of 40 Myr. Hadronic interactions of similarly injected CR protons with the ambient gas should produce an observable gamma-ray signal in accordance with the steady emission of the low state of M87 detected by and H.E.S.S.

sources.summary: The paper studies M87 through archival Chandra monitoring of eight bright X-ray binaries in its population, seeking long-term variability, periodicity, and transient behavior over a decade.

quotes.quote: We report on our search for very-long-term variability (weeks to years) in X-ray binaries (XRBs) in the giant elliptical galaxy M87. We have used archival Chandra imaging observations to characterise the long-term variability of 8 of the brightest members of the XRB population in M87.

Chandra has now studied M87 many times over 10 years; this has the potential for showing long-term modulations or transient behaviour in several XRBs in M87 NGC 4486, D = 17 Mpc; Tully:2008 . We have selected a handful of the brightest sources in this galaxy and searched archival data to study their very long-term variability.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M87 alongside NGC1275 as a cool-core filament object whose observed filament emission can be matched by magnetic reconnection in AGN-bubble wakes.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2012 Cool-core clusters (e.g., Perseus or M87) often possess a network of bright gaseous filaments, observed in radio, infrared, optical and X-ray bands.

For a recent summary of observational results on NGC1275 and M87 filaments, see, e.g., 2011MNRAS.417..172F,2013ApJ...767..153W and references therein. Below we discuss NGC1275 and M87 collectively, under the implicit assumption that the same universal mechanism is responsible for the filamentary structures in both objects (and also in other cool-core clusters).

For the NGC1275 and M87 the estimates of the total emitted surface flux by the filaments are available 2011MNRAS.417..172F,2013ApJ...767..153W : L_ em 10^ 2 ergs s^ 1 cm^ 2 0.2 c_s nkT and 2.2 10^ 3 ergs s^ 1 cm^ 2 0.1 c_s nkT respectively.

sources.summary: The paper confirms slow parsec-scale jet speeds in 1228+126/M87, including a very slow component near the core and non-radial motion slower than HST-1.

quotes.quote: 1228+126 (M87): We confirm the slow speeds we measured in Paper VI for this nearby radio galaxy. The closest component to the core in the main jet (ID = 6) has a very slow pattern speed ( 3.5 4 ), and the fastest component (ID = 4) has significant non-radial motion at 0.026 0.003 . These speeds are significantly slower than those measured in the HST-1 feature ( 0.6 0.3 ; 2010AA...515A..38C ), located more than 80 pc further down the jet. found speeds of 0.25 to 0.4 in the region 2-4 mas downstream from the core, based on five VLBI epochs at 22 and 43 GHz obtained between 1999 and 2004.

sources.summary: The paper studies M87's optical jet with HST archival imaging, measuring knot proper motions, confirming fast inner-jet speeds, and finding transverse and superluminal motions that may trace a helical jet pattern.

quotes.quote: We report new results from an HST archival program to study proper motions in the optical jet of the nearby radio galaxy M87. Using over 13 years of archival imaging, we reach accuracies below 0.1 in measuring the apparent velocities of individual knots in the jet. We confirm previous findings of speeds up to 4.5 in the inner 6 of the jet, and report new speeds for optical components in the outer part of the jet. We find evidence of significant motion transverse to the jet axis on the order of 0.6 in the inner jet features, and superluminal velocities parallel and transverse to the jet in the outer knot components, with an apparent ordering of velocity vectors possibly consistent with a helical jet pattern.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as the Virgo cluster's central radio galaxy with detected gamma-ray emission, possibly linking its low state to cosmic-ray heating of the cool core.

quotes.quote: and H.E.S.S. discovered gamma-ray emission from the radio galaxy M87 (Abdo et al. 2009b, Abramowski et al. 2012), the central galaxy of the Virgo cluster, our closest galaxy cluster at a distance of 17 Mpc. While the TeV emission in the high state is likely connected to the emission from the nucleus/jet, there is the possibility that the low emission state traces pion-decay gamma rays from the Virgo cool-core region as implied by the spectral similarity to LOFAR radio data (see Fig. fig:RGal ). In this picture, the gamma-ray emission can be used to normalize the CR-induced heating rate, which balances that of radiative cooling on average at each radius, thereby suggesting a solution to the ``cooling flow problem'' in the Virgo cluster (Pfrommer 2013). This model would predict the gamma-ray emission in the low state to be steady and slightly extended, which is testable with current observations.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4486, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4486 (M87, Fig. fig:NGC4486 ) is a cD galaxy with a large ( 8 ) core. Within our sample, it is also the galaxy with the largest (apparent) effective radius, 122 as measured from the improved model in which the core is masked. The non-thermal jet as well as central AGN are clearly visible, and were also masked during the analysis.

sources.summary: For M 87, the paper models Suzaku X-ray spectra against complex Virgo-core thermal emission and notes evidence that the core X-rays are jet-dominated.

quotes.quote: Since M 87 is embedded in the bright extended X-ray emission of the Virgo intracluster medium, we took the integration radius as 1 arcmin, and the background spectrum was taken from the 1.5--2.5 arcmin ring, and ignored the HXD-PIN data.

In the case of M 87, good photon statistics allowed us to leave the metal abundance free, but because the temperature structure of the M 87 core region is complex e.g., mat02 , even a two-temperature apec model could not fit the soft X-ray continuum well. Since the detailed investigation of the M 87 temperature structure is beyond the scope of this paper, we simply applied a two-temperature apec model to the 0.7--10 keV range, constraining the main thermal plasma parameters; then the resulting apec model parameter values were fixed during a apec + PL model fit to the 3--10 keV range.

Note that there is already some evidence that the X-ray emission from the M 87 core is dominated by the jet, albeit different jet regions seem to be pronounced at different activity level of the source harr09,harr11 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a radio-galaxy benchmark whose jet has been resolved on gravitational-radius scales and whose black-hole mass matches day-scale gamma-ray variability.

quotes.quote: The record holder is a very long baseline radio interferometry observation of the jet of the nearby radio galaxy M87 at a frequency of 230 GHz, resolving a source with the size of 11.0 0.8 gravitational radii .

An example is the radio galaxy M87 in the Virgo cluster of galaxies . This galaxy harbors a central supermassive black hole with the enormous mass of 6.4 10^9M_ . M87 exhibits gamma-ray variability at a time scale of days which is consistent with the light-crossing time of the event horizon t_ BH ( M87)=0.4 days.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M87 as an elliptical-galaxy EHT target with a central supermassive black hole and jet whose hypothetical plasma blobs could probe strong-gravity spacetime.

quotes.quote: The second target of the EHT is the supermassive black hole at the center of the elliptical galaxy M87. In contrast to Sgr A*, M87 possesses a jet, and it is likely that blobs are ejected along the jet's symmetry axis. In this Letter , we demonstrate that if ejected plasma blobs were detected, one could use their dynamics to probe the spacetime around the black holes. Furthermore, if the mass and spin of a given black hole are known, one can use observations of the blob's dynamics to test general relativity or infer the presence of non-gravitational sources such as gas pressure or magnetic stress.

sources.summary: The review describes M 87 as a nearby Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxy monitored by VERITAS, MAGIC, and H.E.S.S., including a well-sampled 2010 gamma-ray flare.

quotes.quote: Two nearby Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxies have been studied by VERITAS: M 87 ( z=0.004 ) and NGC 1275 ( z=0.018 ), at the center of the Virgo and Perseus galaxy clusters, respectively. The M 87 campaign provides an excellent example of the importance of long-term coordinated monitoring with multiple instruments. The source has been regularly monitored by H.E.S.S., MAGIC and VERITAS over the past decade, and has exhibited several flaring episodes. The most dramatic of these occurred in 2010, when a flare lasting a few days was well-sampled by all three instruments M87_2012 (Fig. M87 ).

sources.summary: The paper measures M87 in a uniform Virgo elliptical nova survey, finding 27 novae, an annual nova rate of 154, and a luminosity-specific rate of 3.8 per 10^10 K-band solar luminosities.

quotes.quote: To explore this idea, relative nova rates in three Virgo elliptical galaxies, M87, M49 and M84, which have significantly different GC specific frequencies ( S_ N ) of 14, 3.6, and 1.6, respectively, were measured over the course of 4 epochs spanning a period of 14 months. To simplify the analysis, observations of the nearly equidistant galaxies were made on the same nights, with the same integration times, and through the same filter (H ), so that the relative numbers of novae discovered would reflect the relative nova rates. At the conclusion of our survey we found a total of 27 novae associated with M87, 37 with M49, and 19 with M84. After correcting for survey completeness, we found annual nova rates of 154^ +23 _ 19 , 189^ +26 _ 22 , and 95^ +15 _ 14 , for M87, M49, and M84, respectively, corresponding to K band luminosity-specific nova rates of 3.8 1.0 , 3.4 0.6 , and 3.0 0.6 novae per year per 10^ 10 L_ K, .

sources.summary: The simulation paper uses M87 as an observed AGN-jet benchmark, citing its conical, then parabolic-to-conical jet shape and stationary HST-1-like recollimation features.

quotes.quote: Some jets appear conical, show large-scale wiggles, decelerate due to the interaction with the ambient medium 1993ASSL..103...95L,2002MNRAS.336.1161L , like the M87 jet 1989ApJ...347..713H .

Here, black hole (BH) powered jets propagate unimpeded, are collimated by the accretion disc wind, and have a parabola-like shape, as seen in the M87 galaxy 2013ApJ...775..118N and the numerical simulations of jet formation mck06jf,hk06,tch11,tch12proc,tch12a,2015ASSL..414...45T . At r_ B , the jet shape is observed to change from parabolic to conical, at least for the M87 galaxy 2013ApJ...775..118N . It is plausible that around this distance the jets start to interact with the ISM/IGM, causing them to undergo a series of recollimations 2015arXiv150802721B that appear as a series of stationary features, such as HST-1, seen in the M87 jet ( 1999ApJ...520..621B ; 2013ApJ...774L..21M ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses M87 as a counter-jet example, citing optical and sub-pc radio detections and noting that its jet-to-counter-jet ratio challenges simple Doppler explanations.

quotes.quote: In the case of M87, an optical counter-jet was reported as early as 1967 Arp1967,Sparks1992 . More recently, radio counter-jet features have been detected in sub-pc scales in the inner jet of M87 ( Kovalev2007 , Walker, Hardee, Davies, Ly, Junor in prep.) However, the measured jet-to-counter-jet ratio (10-15) is inconsistent within the simple Doppler favoritism picture observed with the lack of evidence of fast motions within the inner 20 mas Kovalev2007 . The authors conclude that this may be due to either an intrinsic asymmetry of the jet, or the lack of distinguishable moving features within a smooth fast flowing inner jet.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a massive early-type galaxy example, adopting its SMBH mass, velocity dispersion, effective radius, and shallow core to compute SMBH-binary decay timescales.

quotes.quote: We start by considering the case of a widely studied massive galaxy such as M87, for which M_ 6.15 10^9M_ , 330 km \ s^ 1 2013ARA&A..51..511K , R_e 9 kpc 1995AJ....110.2622L and r_ infl 300 pc 2003ApJ...589L..21M . Given these structural parameters, the calculation of the decay timescales can be simply performed by assuming that the secondary galaxy velocity dispersion is related to the mass of its central SMBH through the M_ relation defined below in Equation ( sigmaz ) setting z=0 FM00,2000ApJ...539L..13G An additional parameter which needs to be defined is , the slope of the deprojected spatial density profile of stars within r_ infl . We adopt the two representative values =0.6 and 1 , as motivated by the facts that the M87 density profile is observed to be nearly flat inside r_ infl and values 0.5 are excluded by our assumption of kinematical isotropy. The left panel of Figure binary-decay gives the orbital decay timescale as a function of the satellite SMBH mass inside M87 at the different stages of the evolution of the massive binary.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a case study for reconstructing AGN jet geometry, emphasizing the jet's complex knots, helical structure, shocks, motions, and optical knot D.

quotes.quote: As an example of the typical knotted structure of AGN jets, we show in Figure fig:vectors-meyer a broad view of the M87 jet, one of the nearest of the class at 17 Mpc distance, taken from 2013ApJ...774L..21M . As can be seen, in even a single image the M87 jet shows an amazing complexity of features, including knots, helical undulations, shocks, and a variety of other things, many of which are oriented at some odd angle with respect to the overall jet direction. Some of these features are seen to move, as shown for the M87 jet by 2013ApJ...774L..21M , who showed that features with apparent velocities up to about 6 are seen within the inner 12.
We ran JetCurry on an optical image of knot D in M87's jet, chosen as an example of small region of a relatively complex jet.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST observations of M87 novae to test whether nova magnitudes converge after maximum light and calibrates two filters for elliptical-galaxy distance work.

quotes.quote: Ten weeks of daily imaging of the giant elliptical galaxy M87 with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) has yielded 41 nova light curves of unprecedented quality for extragalactic cataclysmic variables. We have recently used these light curves to demonstrate that the observational scatter in the so-called Maximum-Magnitude Rate of Decline (MMRD) relation for classical novae is so large as to render the nova-MMRD useless as a standard candle. Here we demonstrate that a modified Buscombe - de Vaucouleurs hypothesis, namely that novae with decline times t_ 2 > 10 days converge to nearly the same absolute magnitude about two weeks after maximum light in a giant elliptical galaxy, is supported by our M87 nova data. For 13 novae with daily-sampled light curves, well determined times of maximum light in both the F606W and F814W filters, and decline times t_ 2 > 10 days we find that M87 novae display M_ 606W,15 = 6.37 0.46 and M_ 814W,15 = 6.11 0.43 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a giant elliptical whose central supermassive black hole is large enough on the sky for EHT imaging and shadow-scale structure tests.

quotes.quote: The SMBHs at the centers of our galaxy (Sgr A*) and the giant elliptical galaxy M87 provide unprecedented opportunities to directly image the innermost regions close to the central black hole, since the angular size of the event horizon is the largest among known black holes. The angular size of the Schwarzschild radius ( R_s ) is 10 as for Sgr A* for a distance of 8.3 kpc and a mass of 4.3 10^6 M_ e.g. chatzopoulos2015 , and 3-7 as for M87 with a distance for 16.7 Mpc blakeslee2009 and a mass of 3-6 10^ 9 M_ e.g. gebhardt2011,walsh2013 .

It corresponds to 52 as for Sgr A* and 16-36 as for M87, which changes by only 4 with the black-hole spin and viewing orientation bardeen1973 . Very long baseline interferometric (VLBI) observations at short/sub-millimeter wavelengths ( 1.3 mm, 230 GHz) can achieve a spatial resolution of a few tens of microarcseconds and therefore are expected to resolve event-horizon-scale structures, including the shadow of SMBHs. Indeed, recent significant progress on 1.3 mm VLBI observations with the Event Horizon Telescope EHT; doeleman2009b has succeeded in resolving compact structures of a few R_s in the vicinity of the SMBHs in both Sgr A* and M87 e.g. doeleman2008,doeleman2012,fish2011,fish2016,akiyama2015,johnson2015 .

sources.summary: NGC 4486 is one of four nearly round slow rotators; unlike the gNFW model, neither MOND interpolation function fits its velocity-dispersion profile acceptably.

quotes.quote: To illustrate, of the 24 \ galaxies we only select galaxies having ellipticities < 0.1 : they are NGC 4486, NGC 4636, NGC 5846, and NGC 6703, and are all slow rotators (see Table 1 of CBS19 and references therein).

For NGC 4486 (top), neither of the IFs provides an acceptable fit. (In contrast, Figure VDgNFW shows that the gNFW does fit well.) Again, at face value, \ of this one galaxy invalidates both the Simple and McGaugh functions as viable MOND IFs.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a giant elliptical benchmark for low-rate black-hole accretion, citing its low gas accretion rate, central black-hole mass, low bolometric luminosity, and possible jet contamination of the observed luminosity.

quotes.quote: The second example is a BH at the center of the giant elliptical galaxy M87. The gas accretion rate at the vicinity of the BH is estimated as 9.2 10^ 4 Kuo_2014 , which is lower than 10^ 2 M _ B Russell_2015 . Since the BH mass is estimated as M_ = 6.6^ +0.4 _ 0.4 10^9 Gebhardt_2011 and M_ = 3.5^ 0.9 _ 0.7 10^9 Walsh_2013 , the bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 2 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 is 3 10^ 7 L_ Edd .

Although the observed luminosity of M87 is several times higher than our estimate, this would be because suppression of the accretion by convective motions in the CDAF regime becomes less efficient for M _ B / M _ Edd 10^ 3 , and/or because there is a level of contamination from the dominant emission due to the jet.

sources.summary: The paper cites M87 as the key nearby case where jet-launching scales can be resolved, discussing spine-sheath structure and possible Blandford-Znajek and disk-jet loading signatures.

quotes.quote: Due to its proximity, M87 is the single exception for which - depending on wavelength - down to 6 Schwarzschild radii can be resolved. Studies of the M87 black hole environment have indicated a spine-sheath structure on pc-scales with about 0.1 pc resolution (see e.g.

Indication for a Blandford-Znajek driving of the M87 jet has been proposed comparing the evolution of observed jet radii along the jet with numerical simulations 2012ApJ...745L..28A, 2016ApJ...833...56A . Recent work suggests that a turbulent mass loading of the disk jet of M87 may be triggered by coronal reconnection events 2017A&A...601A..52B , while 2016A&A...595A..54M recover a two-dimensional velocity field of the 100-1000 pc-scale jet propagation.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as a radio galaxy with multiple high-energy dissipation zones, including HST-1, the inner radio core, and ambiguous 2010 X-ray behavior.

quotes.quote: In the examples above the emitting region is located within the BLR, however, in other cases like M87 different dissipation zones have been found, depending on the event producing the outburst In M87 a long-term MW campaign and very-high energy data have allowed to identify different emitting regions: the TeV flare occurred in 2005 was associated with an X-ray burst of the knot HST-1, at 0.85 '' from the nucleus. In 2008, the coincidence of another TeV flare with radio flares confined the VHE photon production region within 10 Schwarzschild radii. Finally, the 2010 TeV flare was not accompanied by an enhancement of the radio flux but by an increase in the Chandra X-ray flux 3 days after the flare, making hard to unambiguously interpret the data.
Interestingly, a limb-brightened jet structure has been observed in M87 ( ) and recently in NGC 1275 ( , Giovannini 2018, these proceedings).

sources.summary: The paper treats Virgo A (M87) as a nearby radio-galaxy UHECR candidate, finds no current arrival-direction excess, and suggests a past jet flare could explain a Hot Superspot.

quotes.quote: Radiogalaxy Virgo A (M87) in the centre of a cluster of galaxies Virgo Cluster (VC) can be a prominent source of UHECRs. We investigate the possible contribution of Virgo A and the VC to the flux of events with trans-GZK energies -- extremely high energy cosmic rays (EHECRs) -- from the recent Auger and Telescope Array (TA) data sets ( E > 52 EeV and E > 57 EeV , respectively). We simulate EHECR propagation from Virgo A and the VC taking into account their deflections in galactic (GMF) and extragalactic (EGMF) magnetic fields and show that there is no excess of EHECR arrival directions from Virgo A/VC images at different EHECR rigidities.

The Local Filament with its enhanced magnetic field is an expected contributor to the UHECR flux as the closest to Earth last scattering centre, whereas Hot/Cold Spot region is a part of a larger arc-like spot, possibly created by the diffusively spreading jet of UHECRs, accelerated in the relativistic jet of Virgo A during a prominent nuclear outburst about 10 -- 12 Myr ago.

sources.summary: The paper demonstrates VLBI calibration by imaging M87's central radio source, finding a complex edge-brightened jet, possible counter-jet, and speed constraints near the radio core.

quotes.quote: In this work we demonstrate the capabilities of by calibrating and imaging 7 mm Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) data of the central radio source in the M87 galaxy. The reconstructed jet image reveals a complex collimation profile and edge-brightened structure, in accordance with previous results. A potential counter-jet is detected that has 10 of the brightness of the approaching jet. This constrains jet speeds close to the radio core to about half the speed of light for small inclination angles.

sources.summary: The paper models the M87 radio galaxy jet, explaining its observed triple-ridge structure with force-free jet images where relativistic beaming forms the central ridge and magnetic-field effects form the limbs.

quotes.quote: Recent radio VLBI observations of the relativistic jet in M87 radio galaxy have shown a triple-ridge structure that consists of the conventional limb-brightened feature and a central narrow ridge. Motivated by these observations, we examine a steady axisymmetric force-free model of a jet driven by the central black hole (BH) with its electromagnetic structure being consistent with general relativistic magnetohydrodynamic simulations, and find that it can produce triple-ridge images even if we assume a simple Gaussian distribution of emitting electrons at the base of the jet. We show that the fluid velocity field associated with the electromagnetic field produces the central ridge component due to the relativistic beaming effect, while the limb-brightened feature arises due to strong magnetic field around the jet edge which also induces the electrons to be dense there. We also show that the computed image strongly depends on the electromagnetic field structure, viewing angle, and parameters related to the electrons' spatial distribution at the jet base.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M87 deep imaging in Virgo, distinguishing Galactic cirrus from M87 stellar-halo light and tidal streams that lack FUV or infrared emission.

quotes.quote: Even at b=75^ , Virgo has significant contamination from dust at surface brightnesses of _V 27 , visible in optical imaging as broad and diffuse streamers of light to the north, south, and southeast of M87 (near the center of the northern part of the optical image). These features are also seen in both the 250 m and FUV imaging, which show M87 and the Virgo core surrounded by a ring of cirrus. The multiwavelenth data thus acts as a cross-check on the nature of the diffuse optical light: features that lack FUV/IR emission are likely to be diffuse starlight around extragalactic sources. M87's extended stellar halo and the tidal streams to the northwest are all clear of FUV/IR emission, as are the diffuse light features in the southern field around M49; all are bona-fide Virgo stellar light Mihos17 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4486 (M87) as a gamma-ray-detected, radio-bright FRI LLAGN with kpc-scale radio jets in the Palomar sample.

quotes.quote: Three of the 197 AGN analyzed (NGC 315, NGC 1275 and NGC 4486) belong to 4FGL and thus we modelled their spectral shape accordingly to the catalog. These three sources together with NGC 4261 were the only AGN detected in rays ( TS >25 ; see Section results ).

Of the 197 LLAGN in the Palomar sample, only four sources were detected in rays with significance above 5 (TS > 25 ). These four sources correspond to misaligned FRI radio galaxies, each displaying kpc-scale radio jets: NGC 315, NGC 1275, NGC 4261 and NGC 4486 (M87).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra observations of M87's nucleus, decomposes X-ray spectra into disk and flaring components, and explains year-scale variability as accretion of a roughly 0.5 solar-mass cloud.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of year-scale X-ray variation in the nuclear region of the M87 by reanalyze the 8 Chandra observations from 2007 to 2008. The X-ray spectra are fitted and decomposed into disk and flaring components. This year-scale X-ray variability can be explained quite well by a simple clumpy accretion model. We conclude that the central super-massive blackhole of the M87 was accreting a cloud of 0.5 M _ at that time.

sources.summary: The paper cites M87 as the EHT black-hole-shadow galaxy and LLAGN benchmark, noting its asymmetric emission ring and prior self-consistent simulations including radiative interactions.

quotes.quote: Another milestone for high-energy astrophysics were the millimetre-wavelength observations by the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) collaboration, which delivered the first spatially resolved image of a black-hole shadow in the center of the galaxy M87 EHT_M87_PaperI,EHT_M87_PaperV,EHT_M87_PaperVI . This image shows an asymmetric emission ring around the central black hole, which can be explained by the model of a Kerr black hole within the context of general relativity.

Although neglecting the interaction between fluid and the radiation field is a good approximation for a low-luminosity active galactic nuclei (LLAGNs) such as Sgr A*, the inclusion of radiative cooling has been recently considered to produce self-consistent models of another LLAGN such as M87 Moscibrodzka2011,Dibiet12 . Detailed simulations of this source including radiation interaction were carried out recently by Chael2019 .

sources.summary: The paper checks its RIAF electron-temperature model against M87, connecting EHT brightness temperature and photon-ring interpretation to expected hot accretion-flow temperatures near the SMBH.

quotes.quote: The Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration (EHT) reported a horizon-scale image of the SMBH in M87 . Its brightness temperature is 5 10^9 K (equivalent to _e 0.8 ), while the real temperature should be _e 3-10 , because the image is beam-smeared and the RIAF is likely optically thin at the observed frequency. Our model predicts _e 3.5 with the parameters appropriate for M87 ( M=6.3 10^9 M_ , m =6.1 10^ 4 ) , which matches the expected temperature. The electron temperature in RIAFs also affects the interpretation of the photon ring observed by EHT .

sources.summary: The paper describes M87 as a bright radio galaxy whose 10--100 pc jet knots have observed broadband spectra, soft X-ray spectra, possible velocity stratification, and in-situ accelerated electrons.

quotes.quote: M87 and 3C 273 are very bright radio galaxies, and the broadband spectra and velocities of their knots are observed. For M87, the knots in the 10--100 pc scale have soft X-ray spectra without a cutoff feature 2018ApJ...858...27Z . Their intrinsic velocity is estimated to be 0.3-10 with a possible velocity stratification 2019ApJ...887..147P .
For both objects, X-rays are attributed to the in-situ accelerated electrons, which suggests a low value of 300 based on Equation ( eq:eta ) with _j 0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper frames M87 as a nearby giant elliptical radio galaxy with a kpc-scale relativistic jet and models its EHT ring as accretion or jet-base emission.

quotes.quote: The giant elliptical galaxy Messier 87 (M87) is one of the nearest radio galaxies with a prominent relativistic jet extending to several kilo-parsec scales 1989ApJ...340..698O,1996ApJ...473..254S , which has been studied in detail with Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) radio observations 2011Natur.477..185H,2012ApJ...745L..28A,2016A&A...595A..54M,2018ApJ...855..128W and their theoretical modelings 2013ApJ...775..118N,2014ApJ...786....5K,2015ApJ...803...30K,2018ApJ...868..146N . The limb-brightening feature with superluminal blob motions at 15-86 GHz 2007ApJ...668L..27K,2016ApJ...817..131H,2018ApJ...855..128W which is seen down to 50 r_g r_g GM/^2 ; 2018A&A...616A.188K may have hints for driving and emission mechanisms of the jet 2018ApJ...868...82T,2018ApJ...868..146N,2019ApJ...877...19O .

The Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) observed the center of M87 in 2017 with 25 as angular resolution at 230 GHz, and detected the BH shadow surrounded by bright ring-like emission hereafter EHTC2019a, b, , d, e, f 2019ApJ...875L...1E,2019ApJ...875L...2E,2019ApJ...875L...3E,2019ApJ...875L...4E,2019ApJ...875L...5E,2019ApJ...875L...6E . In EHTC2019e, comparing the observational data to theoretical models which combine GRMHD simulations and GR ray-tracing radiative-transfer (GRRT) calculations revealed that the observed ring originates from diffuse, optically-thin synchrotron emission from thermal electrons in the accretion disk and/or “funnel wall” (i.e., the region just outside the funnel) at 4 r_ g .

sources.summary: The paper places M87 among low-luminosity AGN: its normalized line luminosity matches its low Eddington ratio, its massive black hole shows low excitation, and optical/UV data favor a nonthermal continuum.

quotes.quote: For instance, in M87 the normalised line luminosity, (L'_ line / L_ Edd ) = -5.4 (Fig. fig_led ), coincides with the Eddington ratio obtained from the integrated spectral energy distribution, (L_ bol / L_ Edd ) 5.4 1996MNRAS.283L.111R,2016MNRAS.457.3801P .

Likewise, M87 shows a low excitation level (LyH _ IR = 0.8 ) but it hosts one of the most massive BHs of the sample, which might prevent the detection of the accretion disc by this method. However, this disc, if present at all, should be detectable at optical/UV wavelengths, whereas it is not revealed by high angular resolution observations of the nuclear region 2016MNRAS.457.3801P . This supports the non-thermal nature for the ionising continuum (i.e. in agreement with the high LyH _ IR value).

sources.summary: The paper applies a displaced-apex jet model to M87, reproducing the observed bend and parabolic trend and suggesting the inferred jet apex may indicate Blandford-Znajek launching.

quotes.quote: We tested this in application to M87, for which such a bend has been discovered by Hada13 . We find that setting a jet boundary as r_ pc =0.07(z-z_0)^ 0.56 with z_0=0.00153 pc (approximately 2 3 Schwarzschild radii R_ S ) mimics the power law z^ 0.77 on scales of 10-30 R_ s , while following the parabolic trend z^ 0.56 further downstream Hada13 . It is z^ 0.77 that must be used on the scales of the order of z_0 in the expression of the core shift exponent k_z k_distance_acc . This position of an observed jet apex may point to purely Blandford--Znajek mechanism of a jet launch in M87.

sources.summary: The paper models M87 jet observations, describing a parabolic parsec-scale jet that becomes conical near the black hole and shows rapid acceleration at roughly 100 to 1,000 black-hole radii.
