quotes.quote: Recently, very-long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations have revealed the configuration and velocity distribution of the M87 jet near the BH . The parsec-scale jet of the galaxy M87 is parabolic-like , and it becomes conical-like near the BH . The radial profile of the jet velocity was observed by Park et al. using the KVN and VERA Array (KaVA) (KVN: Korean VLBI Network; VERA: VLBI Exploration of Radio Astronomy). The rapid increase in velocity is observed approximately 100 to 1,000 times the BH's radius.

sources.summary: The paper interprets the EHT image of M87’s core, linking the crescent, jet orientation, and apparent rotation to possible retrograde accretion.

quotes.quote: The Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) resolved at 1.3 mm the core M87* of the nearby (distance of D 16.8 Mpc ) radio galaxy Virgo A (M87) into a crescent image of angular diameter of 42 as , which corresponds to 11 gravitational radii R_ g = GM_ BH /^2 for the black hole (BH) mass of M_ BH 6.5 10^9 M_ ( G is the gravitational constant, is the speed of light, M_ is the mass of the Sun; R_ g / 8.9 h ) 2019ApJ...875L...1E,2019ApJ...875L...2E,2019ApJ...875L...3E,2019ApJ...875L...4E,2019ApJ...875L...5E,2019ApJ...875L...6E . M87* is thought to be the base of a relativistic jet observed in radio 1999Natur.401..891J,2007ApJ...663L..65C,2018ApJ...855..128W , optical 1999ApJ...520..621B and X-ray 2002ApJ...568..133W bands, propagating in the WNW direction (position angle 288^ ) beyond the kpc scales. The inclination angle of the jet axis is estimated at 17^ 2018ApJ...855..128W .

However, the counterclockwise (CCW) sense of these rotations is opposite to the clockwise (CW) rotation of the plasma flows implied by the N-S brightness asymmetry, which might indicate that accretion in M87 is retrograde.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M87 as the best northern target for globular-cluster FRBs, predicting about ten sources and high detection probability because of its unusually large cluster system.

quotes.quote: Of all local galaxies, we find M87 is the best candidate for FRB detections. We predict M87's globular cluster system contains O (10) FRB sources at present and that a dedicated radio survey (by FAST) of O (10) hr has a 90 probability of detecting a globular cluster FRB in M87.

Given that M87 has by far the most globular clusters ( N_ cl 10^4 ) of any nearby galaxy (see column 6 of Table table:rates ), this is not a surprise. Although Cen A contains a factor of roughly 10 fewer clusters relative to M87, its relative proximity enables lower luminosity bursts to be detected.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as a nearby FRI source whose jet X-rays are synchrotron, highlights the HST-1 outburst, and excludes it from the variability sample because it is already deeply studied.

quotes.quote: Assuming a leptonic jet model, the X-ray emission of most low-power or FRI class sources, including nearby sources such as M87 harris2003_sed and Cen A hardcastle2007 , are often (but not always) well-described by synchrotron radiation from a single electron population which extends from radio to optical and X-ray energies.

While variability had been observed by this point in nearby FRI jets most dramatically in the mid-2000s outburst of HST-1 in the jet of M87; harris2003 variability on this timescale was completely unexpected.

Two sources (3C 305 and 3C 171) were excluded as the extended X-ray emission associated has been attributed to jet-driven gas hardcastle2012_3c305,hardcastle2010_3c171 ; we also exclude the sources M87 and Centaurus A, which are already known to produce X-rays by synchrotron process feigelson1981,kraft2002,wilson2000 . Both M87 and Centaurus A have been far more deeply and frequently observed than typical of the remaining sample (with 50 and 43 distinct observations, respectively), and this is likely one of the reasons that X-ray variability has been reported in both cases.

sources.summary: The review discusses M87 as the host galaxy of M87*, whose massive black hole enables horizon-scale EHT studies and powers a famous jet that heats the surrounding galaxy.

quotes.quote: This perspective will focus primarily on two of the most well-studied supermassive BHs: the 4 10^6 M_ BH, called Sagittarius A* (Sgr A*), at the center of our own Galaxy , and the 6 10^9 M_ BH, called M87* , at the center of the galaxy M87.
For our purposes here, Sgr A* and M87* are important primarily because they are nearby by astronomical standards, enabling particularly detailed studies, e.g., Figure fig:EHT_images .

The prototypical example is M87*, which has a famous jet (see Figure fig:M87jet ) whose power exceeds the radiative power of the BH's accretion disk. The energy carried by such jets far away from the BH and into the surrounding galaxy turns out to be a major source of heating that strongly influences how the surrounding galaxy grows.

sources.summary: The paper notes Messier 87's EHT black-hole shadow, X-ray emission and variability, collisionless LLAGN accretion flow, and model-discriminating observed X-ray flux.

quotes.quote: Messier 87 (M87), now famous for the first picture of a black hole shadow by the event horizon telescope collaboration (EHT) eht-paperI , is also active in X-ray emissions Wilson2001,Marshall2002,perlman2005,prieto2016 , and shows X-ray variability on timescales of days Harris2009 .

However, accretion flows in LLAGN like M87* and SgrA* have a mean free path for the electrons that is much large than the actual system size, making them effectively collisionless, and deviations from thermality are, therefore, to be expected.
For M87, the X-ray emission is a clear discriminator between models. If the electron temperature in the accretion disk is too high, the model easily overproduces the observed X-ray flux.

sources.summary: The paper treats Virgo galaxy (M87) as a dark-matter-halo lensing example, giving its black-hole mass, distance, halo parameters, and comparing its Einstein-ring radius with other galaxies.

quotes.quote: Here, we restrict ourselves to the Burkert model to show the Einstein ring angular radius for gravitational lensing in the Milky Way, Andromeda galaxy(M31), Virgo galaxy(M87), and ESO138-G014 galaxy.

In the Virgo galaxy the mass of the supermassive black hole M87 is M=6.5 10^9M_ , the distance between the observer and the lens plane is D_ OL =16.8 Mpc, the characteristic radius and density of the dark matter halo are h=91.2 kpc, _0=6.9 10^6M_ /kpc^3 .

As shown in Table Table galaxy , by taking D_ LS /D_ OS as 0.2 , 0.5 , and 0.8 respectively, we can obtain the angular radius values of Einstein rings in different galaxies. And we can see that the gravitational lensing of Milky Way has the largest angular radius of the Einstein ring, followed by the Virgo galaxy(M87), the ESO138-G104 galaxy smaller, and the Andromeda galaxy(M31) smallest.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as the Virgo central galaxy whose EHT shadow, distance, SMBH mass, and polarization images support the black-hole shadow interpretation.

quotes.quote: The EHT has also detected the predicted shadow in the massive central galaxy of the Virgo galaxy cluster, M87 (or Virgo A) akiyama2019 . The distance of M87 is 16.8 Mpc, 2000 times further away than SgrA*. Since its mass of the central SMBH is 1500 larger than that of SgrA*, 6.2 - 6.5 10^ 9 M_ , the diameter of its shadow is 42 3 micro-arcsec, comparable to that of SgrA*, and strengthening further the `shadow' interpretation. Detailed polarization images of M87 with the EHT that the near-event horizon magnetic field structure is poloidal and the region is magnetically dominated, as in SgrA* akiyama2021a, akiyama2021b .

sources.summary: The paper cites M 87 as a radio galaxy whose 2005 high-energy flare arose from HST-1 at 120 pc, while a 2012 high state came from the core.

quotes.quote: A remarkable example is the radio galaxy M 87. The high resolution of radio and X-rays observations discovered the jet knot HST-1, at 120 pc from the AGN, as the locus of the high-energy emission observed in 2005 cheung07,harris2009 . Instead, the high activity state observed in 2012 originated at the source core, whereas HST-1 remained quiescent hada14 .

We notice that at the redshift of PKS 1127 145 the X-ray flaring component observed in the jet of Pictor A would be at about 4 arcsec from the core (i.e. closer than component O), while M87 HST-1 would be at 12 milliarcsecond from the core, in the innermost pc-scale structure.

sources.summary: The paper uses ROSAT spectra of M87 to argue for intrinsic oxygen absorption and warm multiphase gas extending across the galaxy system.

quotes.quote: M87 is arguably the best target for study of its hot plasma with ROSAT because it is the brightest nearby galaxy, group, or cluster that also possesses an ambient gas temperature ( 2 keV) that lies within the bandpass of the PSPC. From analysis of spatially resolved, deprojected ROSAT PSPC spectra we find the strongest evidence to date for intrinsic oxygen absorption and multiphase gas in the hot ISM/IGM/ICM of a galaxy, group, or cluster. When attempting to describe the 0.2-2.2 keV ROSAT emission of M87 by a single-phase hot plasma modified by standard Galactic absorption the best-fitting model displays striking residuals in the spectrum (Figure fig ): (1) excess emission above the model for 0.2-0.4 keV and (2) excess absorption below the model for 0.5-0.8 keV. These features are apparent out to the largest radii investigated ( 100 kpc) and cannot be attributed to errors in the calibration or the background subtraction.

sources.summary: The paper uses XMM-Newton to study M87's thermal X-ray halo, lower-temperature extensions, radio-lobe environment, and jet-driven energy input in the Virgo Cluster core.

quotes.quote: The high signal to noise and good point spread function of XMM have allowed the first detailed study of the interaction between the thermal and radio emitting plasma in the central regions of M87. We show that the X-ray emitting structure, previously seen by ROSAT, is thermal in nature and that the east and southwest extensions in M87's X-ray halo have a significantly lower temperature (k T= 1.5 keV) than the surrounding ambient medium (k T= 2.3 keV). There is little or no evidence for non-thermal emission with an upper limit on the contribution of a power law component of spectral index flatter than 3 being less than 1 of the flux in the region of the radio lobes.

In the same region of space a pair of relativistic jets is emerging from the active nucleus of M87, ejecting energy into this environment at an estimated rate of 10 ^ 44 ergs s ^ 1 in the form of relativistic plasma (e.g.

sources.summary: The paper discusses dynamical modeling of M87, where a spherical mass-follows-light model matched photometry and dispersion but became unstable in N-body tests.

quotes.quote: For example, Newton Binney (1984) successfully constructed a distribution function that could reproduce the photometric and kinematic properties of M87: assuming only that the mass of the galaxy were distributed in the same way as its light and that the galaxy is spherical, they were able to match both the light distribution of M87 and the variation in its line-of-sight velocity dispersion with projected radius. Thus, they would appear to have a completely viable dynamical model for M87. However, Merritt (1987) took this distribution function as the starting point for an N-body simulation, and showed that the preponderance of stars on radial orbits at its centre rendered the model unstable -- the N-body model rapidly formed a bar at its centre. Thus, the simple spherical model in which the mass followed the light was invalidated, implying either that M87 is not intrinsically spherical, or that it contains mass in addition to that contributed by the stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4486 as M87, the central Virgo elliptical, with an exceptionally rich globular-cluster system, well-defined bimodality, and spectroscopic constraints on cluster metallicities and kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486 As the Virgo central elliptical galaxy (M87) it lies at the center of the Virgo cluster gravitational potential and X--ray emission, although it is moving slightly with respect to the cluster center of mass velocity as determined from galaxy velocities huc85 . Its GC system is exceptionally rich with over 10,000 GCs and a S _N 14 ash98 . HST observations have allowed the bimodality to be well defined els96 . Spectroscopic studies have derived metallicities and kinematics for a large number of M87 GCs mou87,hb87,coh98 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as an example of a central velocity-dispersion drop, contrasting its 350 km/s central value with the Virgo cluster and applying a concentration scaling to satellite-halo corrections.

quotes.quote: M87 provides a good example of this. This galaxy lies at the center of the Virgo cluster and has a central velocity dispersion of 350 km/s whereas the cluster has a global value that is a factor of two larger. If we assume that the dSph's are similar to M87, then the correction should scale roughly as the concentration parameter. Since c_ M87 /c_ dSph 0.4-0.5 , then we expect the maximum correction to v_ peak to be an increase of 50 over our quoted values.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a nearby powerful AGN source candidate for ultra-high-energy cosmic rays, but notes expected arrival-direction clustering and magnetic-wind complications.

quotes.quote: If M87 is the primary source of UHECRs a concentration of events in the direction of M87 should be seen. The next known nearby source after M87 is NGC315 which is already too far at a distance of 80 Mpc. Any unknown source between M87 and NGC315 would likely contribute a second hot spot, not the observed isotropic distribution.

In particular, a strong Galactic wind can significantly alter the paths of UHECRs such that the observed arrival directions of events above 10 ^ 20 eV would trace back to the North Galactic Pole which is close to the Virgo cluster where M87 resides .

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC4486 as having an unusual central blue depression in its colour profile, probably associated with its conspicuous non-thermal jet and affected by seeing.

quotes.quote: NGC4486 is remarkable in showing a central ''blue deep", the U B colour beeing 0.51 at =0 as compared to 0.72 near = 6.5 . This central feature is probably somehow related to the famous non-thermal jet of this galaxy. The jet is of course very conspicuous in U B , with a peak colour near -0.1. Needless to say, both the central ''blue deep" and the jet are affected by seeing (and our attempts to correct its effects).

sources.summary: The paper treats M 87 as a giant elliptical benchmark with a massive nuclear black hole and finds its required cluster density for AGN accretion is comparatively normal-low.

quotes.quote: Consider three well studied galaxies having a firm evidence of presence of a nuclear b.h.: our Milky Way, M 31 and the giant elliptical M 87 in Virgo. The modern estimates of the central black hole masses available in the literature are, respectively, 2.6 10^6 M _ , 6.2 10^7 M _ and 3.6 10^9 M _ .

Assuming <v_*> =10 kms ^ 1 for we get _c= 3.4 10^7 M _ pc ^ 3 for the MW, _c=5 10^5 M _ pc ^ 3 for M 31 and _c=300 M _ for M 87. The value of _c for M87 is clearly a normal-low value for the central density of galactic GCs, while the _c of M 31, even if large, is compatible with the estimated central values of some non core-collapsed GCs (see the Harris web Catalog of parameters for Milky Way globular clusters, feb. 2003, http://physun.physics.mcmaster.ca/ harris/mwgc.dat).

sources.summary: The review cites M 87 as a nearby radio galaxy where jet knots show shock acceleration, optical and X-ray variability, and wavelength-dependent polarization structures.

quotes.quote: In M 87 there is evidence for strong shock acceleration at the base of bright emitting regions, in compressed transverse magnetic fields . A knot in the jet of M 87 has been observed to vary in the X-ray and optical on the timescale of months, consistent with shock acceleration, expansion, and energy losses .

A particularly good example is M 87, where the use of HST to map the optical polarization provides additional information, since the lifetimes of the electrons emitting optical synchrotron emission are far shorter than those of radio-emitting electrons. Significant differences between the polarization structures seen in the optical and radio in M 87 suggest that the sites of acceleration are different for different electron energies, with the strongest shocks, that provide acceleration to the highest energies, appearing in the most central parts of the jet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4486 as a large peculiar elliptical with strong X-ray emission and treats it as a CUM Sink associated with Virgocentric infall.

quotes.quote: aaro2 found the peculiar velocity field in the local supercluster is directed toward NGC 4486 (Messier 087) with ( l, b) (284^ , 74^ ) at a speed of 331 41 km s ^ 1 . This has been called the ``Virgocentric infall''. NGC 4486 is a peculiar, large, elliptical galaxy with strong X-ray emissions.
Therefore, the inward peculiar velocity field of the Virgocentric infall is because NGC 4486, NGC 5128, and Centaurus B were considered Sinks with mass surrounding them.

sources.summary: The paper uses gamma-ray upper limits for the M 87 kpc-scale jet to constrain its magnetic field, arguing it is at least equipartition strength and may need amplification.

quotes.quote: Here we show that upper limits on the high-energy and very high-energy ray emission of the kpc-scale jet in M 87 radio galaxy imposed by EGRET , Whipple , and --- most importantly --- HEGRA and HESS observations, provide important constraints on the magnetic field strength in this object. In particular, a non-detection of ray radiation from the brightest part of this jet (knot A), expected from the inverse-Compton scattering of the starlight photons by the synchrotron-emitting jet electrons, implies that the magnetic field cannot be smaller than the equipartition value (referring solely to the radiating ultrarelativistic electrons), and most likely, is even stronger. In this context, we point out several consequences of the obtained result for the large-scale jet structures in FR I radio galaxies and the M 87 jet in particular. For example, we discuss a potential need for amplification of the magnetic field energy flux along these jets (from sub-pc to kpc scales), suggesting a turbulent dynamo as a plausible process responsible for the aforementioned amplification.

sources.summary: NGC 4486 is a Virgo comparison elliptical whose globular-cluster colour distribution is bimodal, with red clusters significantly redder than those in NGC 4365.

quotes.quote: The homogeneity and stability of HST data allow us to carry out a robust comparison of GCs in NGC 4365 with three other large Virgo ellipticals with rich GC systems: NGC 4406 (M86), NGC 4486 (M87) and NGC 4649 (M60).
NGC 4365, NGC 4486 and NGC 4649 have similar colours, while NGC 4406 is bluer in both \ and .

NGC 4486 and NGC 4649 also show bimodal colour distributions with blue peaks at about the same colour as in NGC 4365 or perhaps slightly redder. The red peaks in these two galaxies are significantly redder than in NGC 4365.

sources.summary: The paper traces M87's diffuse halo to 175 kpc, describing an extended irregular envelope with boxy isophotes, loops, and shells indicating an unrelaxed halo.

quotes.quote: At low surface brightness, the giant elliptical M87 shows a very extended and irregular stellar envelope. From photographic images, Weil (1997) and Katsiyannis (1998) show M87's luminous halo extending to 100 kpc scales; on our image it can be traced further out to 175 kpc (I). Here the outer isophotes are not simply the radial extension of M87's inner regular luminosity profile; traced to the northwest, the isophotes become boxier and show irregular loops and shells which may be remnants from earlier stripping from, or cannibalization of, smaller galaxies. The lopsidedness and irregularity of M87's halo is again indicative of a diffuse envelope which is not fully relaxed.

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra observations of M87 to describe its X-ray jet, cavities, rings, plumes, and outbursts from the central supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: The left portion of Figure f:m87 shows the X-ray image one of one of these galaxies, M87 (also a Messier catalog object). Bright arcs and dark cavities in the multimillion degree gas of M87 accompany the central jet. Further away, faint rings with two spectacular plumes extend beyond the jet. These features are evidence of repetitive outbursts from the vicinity of the central supermassive black hole which impact the entire galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a benchmark galaxy where adiabatic black-hole-growth models applied to HST photometry agree with kinematic black-hole masses and direct profile fitting.

quotes.quote: These models have been used to interpret HST photometry for two well-studied galaxies that also have kinematically determined BH masses, namely M87 and M32. Given that observed surface brightness cusps can be interpreted in so many ways, it is quite remarkable how successful the models have been for these two galaxies. The photometrically determined BH mass for M87 (Young 1978; Lauer 1992a) agrees within the errors to that determined kinematically (Harms 1994; Macchetto 1997), as is the case for M32 (Lauer 1992b; van der Marel 1998).

This was verified explicitly for the case of M87, for which the BH mass inferred by fitting to the data directly differs by only 0.04 dex from the BH mass inferred by fitting to the nuker law parameterization.

sources.summary: The paper cites M87 as a nearby FRI radio source with a massive black hole and low accretion relative to its Eddington limit.

quotes.quote: For example, studies of the gas dynamics of the inner regions of M87 imply a supermassive black hole with M_ BH = 3 10^ 9 M_ , (Ford et al. 1996; Maccheto et al. 1997).

For example M87 now hosts an FRI radio source with L_ 151 10^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 sr ^ 1 and Q_ phot 10^ 37 W, thus putting it at the lower-left corner of Fig. fig:model . However, the dynamical evidence is that it contains one of the most massive black holes known, M_ BH = 3 10^ 9 M_ . Therefore it must be accreting at well below its Eddington limit.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a radio galaxy where photon-axion conversion might affect very-high-energy emission, citing its variable core, flaring knots, magnetic fields, and low accretion state.

quotes.quote: Among the extragalactic objects which have been seen in very high energy rays and where photon-axion conversion could occur are Markarian 421 , Markarian 501 , the blazar 1ES 1101-232 , and the variable core and flaring knots of M87.

Magnetic fields of order 10 G in the vicinity of the radio galaxy M87 have been discussed in Ref. . This value is consistent with the estimate Eq. ( eq:B_agn ) if M87 is in a low accretion state, f_ Edd 10^ 6 , which is the case according to present measurements and theories .

sources.summary: The paper describes M87 as a nearby Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxy detected at VHE gamma rays, with fast variability implying emission from a compact black-hole-scale region in a misaligned jet system.

quotes.quote: The Fanaroff-Riley type I radio galaxy M87 ( z=0.0043 ) has been detected at VHE gamma-rays by HESS (Aharonian et al. ), confirming a previous detection reported by HEGRA. The observed fast variations indicate that the emission comes from a region with a dimension similar to the Schwarzschild radius of the central black hole. These observations confirm that TeV gamma-rays are emitted by extragalactic sources other than blazars, where jets are not relativistically beamed towards the observer.

sources.summary: M87 is cited as an FR I radio galaxy whose core lacks a dusty torus and is dominated by non-thermal jet emission.

quotes.quote: This suggestion was corroborated by who demonstrated the absence of a dusty torus in M87, one of the sources in the FR I sample, by placing stringent limits on its thermal IR emission. Observations by further solidified this conclusion. The IR radiation they detect in the M87 core comes primarily from non-thermal jet emission, with only a trace of thermal emission that is much weaker than what would be expected from an AGN torus and that can be attributed to neighboring dust. The behavior displayed by M87 appears to be common in FR I sources.

sources.summary: The review identifies M87 as a black-hole lensing target, giving its central black-hole mass, shadow radius, millimeter observations, and interest from its jet structure.

quotes.quote: For example, M87 has a central black hole of 3.2 10^9 M_ at a distance of 15 Mpc from us , resulting in a shadow radius of 11 as.

Observations of M87 at this wavelength have also been carried out . This has inspired the idea that the black hole in M87 could be an even more interesting target than Sgr A* , thanks to its jet structure.

sources.summary: The paper cites M87 as a LINER case study where circumnuclear disk spectra can be modeled with shocks while inner gas may transition from photoionization to shocks.

quotes.quote: Perhaps the role of shocks can be better understood by considering the case studies of the nucleus and circumnuclear disk of M87 based on spectra obtained through small (sub-arcsecond) apertures with the . According to dopita96b and dopita97 the optical+UV emission-line spectrum of the circumnuclear gaseous disk of M87 can be explained by models invoking fast ( 200 ) shocks. After studying the inner regions of the disk of M87, sabra03 conclude that the gas is excited by photons from the active nucleus but they also note that there appears to be a transition from photoionization to shock excitation at a distance of a few tens of parsec from the center.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Virgo core around M87 using planetary-nebula velocities, separating its extended halo from intracluster light and linking negative-velocity debris to stripping from the M86 subgroup.

quotes.quote: From the positions and velocities of the detected PNe a projected phase-space diagram was built which illustrates the different dynamical components along the LOS in the Virgo cluster core, see Fig. fig:virgophsp : these are the extended halo of M87 and the ICL component. Within R< 3600'' = 260 kpc from M87, the ICL component is all at negative velocities relative to M87, and it is not phase-mixed. Part of this component is at velocities consistent with the idea that light of the M86 sub-group is being tidally stripped by the more massive M87 component, while the two galaxies approach each other along the LOS.

sources.summary: The paper examines M87's outer halo with Spitzer imaging, finding a flat or blue color gradient to 160 kpc and colors consistent with old stellar populations rather than dust effects.

quotes.quote: We also examined the outer halo of the giant elliptical M87. The color profile from the inner to outer halo of M87 (160 Kpc) is consistent with either a flat or optically blue gradient, where a blue gradient could be due to younger or lower metallicity stars at larger radii. The similarity of the age predicted by both the infrared and optical colors ( > few Gyr) indicates that the optical measurements are not strongly affected by dust extinction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4486 as M87, a cD elliptical in Virgo, and highlights discrepant Sersic-index estimates caused by extra halo light and 2D profiling.

quotes.quote: NGC4486 (M87) is the second brightest elliptical Virgo galaxy and classified as a cD due to extra halo light originating from the cluster. tex:FC06a and GD07 agreed on their S e rsic index measurements 6.1 (using S e rsic-core model) and 6.8 respectively while KF09 profiled with the extra halo and found n=11.86 . A big discrepancy in the S e rsic values appears when we transferred to two dimensional profiling where both tex:D01 and H09 found a significantly low S e rsic index n=3.0 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a Bologna Complete Sample radio galaxy detected by Fermi despite small core dominance, attributing its gamma-ray detectability to proximity rather than favorable jet orientation.

quotes.quote: As expected, among the 1FGL Radiogalaxies (RG), the majority of sources are above the Giovannini 1994's correlation (GC) having high CD which indicates small angle ( ) of view; M87 and Cen A are peculiar objects being below the GC while 3C 236 is interesting being not in the 1FGL Catalog (see Notes on sources below);
Three objects have peculiar behavior and need notes: M87 has small CD and large but it is detected by Fermi as a consequence of its proximity (z=0.04).
Two sources are detected by Fermi despite their small CD: M87 because of its proximity, and CenA for which the gamma-ray emission comes from the lobes.

sources.summary: The paper notes M87 is a bright Virgo galaxy but lacks observed UHECR clustering, a discrepancy it says drives rejection of AGN dominance with small smearing angles and may relate to luminosity.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, no such clustering is seen around M87 which is one of the brightest galaxies in the Virgo cluster. This discrepancy may be a main cause for our main result that both CADD method rules out AGN dominance with small smearing angles. Zaw et al. suggested that this lack of observed UHECR around M87 can be explained by considering the bolometric luminosity .

sources.summary: The review identifies M 87 as a TeV radio galaxy and highlights a multi-year, multi-instrument campaign that detected several flaring episodes in this nearby non-blazar AGN.

quotes.quote: 3 radio galaxies have been detected at TeV energies (Cen A, M 87 and NGC 1275).

The most impressive of those is the multi-year campaign on M 87 that involved VERITAS, MAGIC, H.E.S.S. (80 hours in total), the Fermi-LAT, Chandra, the HST and various other instruments all the way down to 1.7 GHz VLBA measurements (see Figure m87 ). 3-4 flaring episodes were detecting in these observations that provide a rich data set to study rise-and decay-times and spectral properties of flares in this close-by non-blazar AGN.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a giant-elliptical example, arguing that its black hole influence radius and long cluster-inspiral times make nuclear star cluster formation inefficient.

quotes.quote: In order to do so, we repeat a similar analysis to that presented in MW but for a galaxy like M87, a giant elliptical with no evidence for .

The influence radius of the \ in a galaxy like M87 extends much further out (a few hundreds of parsecs from the center) than the tidal disruption radius of a massive globular cluster ( 10 pc), while for the MW we found r_ disr r_ infl .
6 For globular clusters orbiting in the core of a massive elliptical galaxy like M87, the timescale to reach the center is much longer than one Hubble time (Figure Fig4 ).

sources.summary: The paper compares M87 with M49, describing M87 as Virgo's central giant elliptical with higher Sersic index, flatter outer color gradient, and continuing satellite-driven halo and ICL growth.

quotes.quote: It is interesting in this context to compare M49 to M87, the giant elliptical at the heart of Virgo. While the two galaxies are comparable in luminosity, M87 has a higher Sersic index ( n=11.8 , compared to M49's n=6.0 ; Kormendy 2009) and flatter color gradient (Rudick 2010) at large radius than M49. Where it sits, M87 is also subject to a constant bombardment of satellite galaxies; this dynamically active environment continually adds material to M87's outer halo, as well as the ICL around M87 (Mihos 2005), resulting in the more extended envelope with relatively high mean metallicity.

sources.summary: The atlas gives M87's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: M87 is the central giant elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster at a distance of D= 16.7 2.6 Mpc with a FR I radio morphology, a radio-loud LINER nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and a prominent jet visible at all wavelengths (see meisenheimer_synchrotron_1996 for a summary on the jet properties). It contains one of the most massive black hole of the local galaxy population ( M_ BH /M_ = 9.82 0.03 ; gebhardt_black_2011 ). The first MIR photometry of M87 was attempted by but the nucleus remained undetected in N- and Q band.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4486 with Kormendy profiles, identifies it as one of two galaxies with mass deficits above ten black hole masses, and notes its cD-halo fitting issue.

quotes.quote: For six galaxies whose surface brightness profiles are taken from Kormendy-09a (i.e., NGC 4261, NGC 4374, NGC 4472, NGC 4486, NGC 4552, and NGC 4649), the best-fitting \ model of Kormendy-09a is also overplotted on the right-hand panels of Fig. fittingplots .
The next-largest mass deficit is 18 times , in NGC 4261, with the one other galaxy having a mass deficit larger than 10 \ being NGC 4486.

In addition, in the particular case of NGC 4486, Kormendy-09b used a S ersic fit (from Kormendy-09a , using semi-major axis) which excluded data outside 400 , because it apparently gave rise to a S ersic index which was too large (almost 12) due to the cD halo.

sources.summary: M87 is cited as a nearby AGN whose VLBA-imaged relativistic jet opening angle, streamline shape, and broad inner radio emission resemble the paper's collimated MHD wind models.

quotes.quote: For example, a nearby AGN, M87, is known to exhibit pronounced relativistic jets ( 5-10 ) in the form of a series of cores and knots imaged by the VLBA e.g. Junor99,Hada11,Asada12 ; Angular resolution of VLBA measurements has enabled us to trace through the jet streamlines all the way back to the nucleus region at /r_o 10 allowing for constraining the launching site (whether from the ergosphere or accretion disk). Being launched with an initial opening angle of 60 as estimated in the observed paraboloidal streamline within 100 r_s , the asymptotic shape of the observed jets are characterized by a conical shape with an opening angle of 6 Owen89 in consistent with our fiducial winds, for example, models (A) and (D), where _ open,4 7 and 6 , respectively. While still uncertain in understanding of local complexities such as subcomponents, the geometry of the inner jets at /r_o 10-100 appears to be rapidly converging to the base in a way similar to the model (A). For example, the mildly-relativistic innermost layer of such disk-winds thus may account for spatially-broad radio emission detected in M87 e.g. McKinney06 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies M87 as the only elliptical Planck-detected Virgo galaxy and says its far-infrared/submillimetre emission is synchrotron-dominated rather than dust-dominated.

quotes.quote: The only elliptical galaxy detected at 857 GHz is M87, the massive radio galaxy at the centre of the Virgo Cluster. Whether or not this galaxy has a substantial interstellar dust reservoir detectable at FIR/submm wavelengths is still a matter of debate, even with the Herschel data at hand 2010A&A...518L..53B, 2010A&A...518L..61B, 2013A&A...552A...8D . It is clear, however, that the emission at frequencies of 857 GHz and lower is dominated by synchrotron emission that goes as S_ ^ with a spectral index 0.75 2007ApJ...655..781S, 2009ApJ...705..356B, 2009ApJ...701.1872C, 2010A&A...518L..53B .

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as the central Virgo galaxy near HVGC-1, comparing the object to M87 velocities and considering ejection by M87 substructure or a binary SMBH.

quotes.quote: In fact, the unusual velocity does not appear to be consistent with the tail of the GC velocity distribution of any individual Virgo galaxy, including M87. For M87 in particular the velocity dispersion at this projected radius is about 300 \ (Strader 2011a), so HVGC-1 would be a more than 7 outlier. We must look for other mechanisms to explain its relative velocity of over 2300 \ with respect to M87.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as the massive Virgo giant elliptical comparison case, citing its high central velocity dispersion, lower stellar density, and survival of wider core binaries than in smaller ellipticals.

quotes.quote: Now, let us consider two relatively extreme giant elliptical galaxies: M87, the central galaxy in the Virgo Cluster, and NGC 4458, a small galaxy in the Virgo Cluster. For M87, the central velocity dispersion is about 400 km/sec, and the central density is about 200 stars per cubic parsec (Gebhardt Thomas 2009). For NGC 4458, the central density is about 2800 L_ /pc ^3 (Gebhardt et al. 1996), and the central velocity dispersion in 85 km/sec (van Dokkum Conroy 2011). In general, the fundamental plane relation (Dressler et al. 1987) shows giant elliptical galaxies which fit on the plane will become significantly denser and have significantly lower velocity dispersions as their masses drop. For a 10 Gyr old population in a dynamical environment like that of M87, binary separations of up to about 20 AU will be possible in the core; for a galaxy like NGC 4458, binary separations of about 0.4 AU will be possible.

sources.summary: The survey uses M87 as an example where 2MASS underestimates total K-band luminosity, motivating deeper photometry for massive extended early-type galaxies.

quotes.quote: M87, for instance, has M_K=-25.31 mag according to the 2MASS XSC and is only slightly above our magnitude cut. Analysis of deeper photometric data, however, finds M_K = -26.08 mag, a factor of two more luminous lasker2014a . Although M87 is likely to be a worst case because of its angular extent on the sky, this large discrepancy underscores the need for deeper K band imaging data.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a principal EHT target: a supergiant elliptical with a prominent optical jet and a black hole large enough in angular size for event-horizon-scale observations.

quotes.quote: Both the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way, Sagittarius A*, and that of the elliptical galaxy M87 are massive enough and close enough (subtending tens of microarcseconds) for the EHT to resolve. Sgr A* is predicted to subtend a larger angle on the sky, and with a declination of 29^ , it remains permanently visible in the Southern Hemisphere. However, radio images of Sgr A* are blurred due to interstellar scattering by turbulent electrons bac78,bow06 , which is consistent with a thin scattering screen located near the Scutum spiral arm bow14 . Supergiant elliptical galaxy M87, known for its striking jet in the optical, provides an alternate source--it has a black hole nearly as large in angular size, and without any indication of strong interstellar scattering at e.g., had11 . Initial EHT observations have made the first detections of event horizon scale structure using both sources doe08,fis11,doe12 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a benchmark BCG-type globular-cluster system, noting its 13,000 clusters, published GCLF parameters, and little turnover-luminosity change out to 40 kpc.

quotes.quote: The paradigm of a GC system in a BCG-type galaxy is that in M87, which holds 13,000 clusters e.g. harris09b .
peng_etal2009 , using the same F814W bandpass for extremely deep HST data around M87, find log L_0 = 5.06 0.02 and _I = 0.55 0.02 dex with an excellent fit to a log-normal model (see their Figure 8).
By contrast, tamura_etal06 and jordan2007 find little change in the turnover luminosity out to R 40 kpc for the Virgo giant M87.

sources.summary: 3C 274/M87/Virgo A has a poor SED fit from high 2MASS luminosities, better WISE agreement, and very low redshift.

quotes.quote: 3C 274 (M87, Virgo A). The poor fit with a very high ^2 value is due to the systematically higher 2MASS luminosities. WISE data appear to agree better with 2MASS, however. Like 3C 272.1, this source is at very low redshift (z=0.0043).

sources.summary: The paper selects Virgo A for UHECR image simulations, finding its deflection arc useful and its high-latitude feature capable of identifying it as the point source.

quotes.quote: From ten studied local radiogalaxies the arrival deflection follows a single arc and the deflection can be described by the inverse linear fit below R=10 EV only for two of them, Virgo A and NGC 4261.
For further investigation of the UHECR image we have selected the radiogalaxy Virgo A.
This unique feature is due to high galactic latitude of Virgo A and can be used for an identification of Virgo A as the UHECR point source.

sources.summary: The paper constrains NGC 4486 (M87) as a massive black-hole host, finding any PTA-band SMBH binary companion needs a few-percent-or-smaller mass ratio.

quotes.quote: For the black holes with 5 10^9 M_ at the centers of NGC 1600 , NGC 4889, NGC 4486 (M87) and NGC 4649 (M60), any binary companion in orbit within the PTA frequency bands would have to have a mass ratio of a few percent or less .

Figure fig:ratio_constraint shows our resulting upper limits on the SMBH binary mass ratio as a function of GW frequency for NGC 1600 (orange curves), NGC 4889 (red curves), NGC 4486 (M87; purple curves), and NGC 4649 (M60; blue curves).

Therefore, even if there is a very unequal-mass SMBH binary in NGC 1600, NGC 4889, NGC 4486, or NGC 4649, its orbit will likely be highly eccentric and render a detection improbable in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper treats Virgo A as a bright absolute radio source with arcminute-scale structure, but says its 16-arcminute extent makes it too large for most interferometer calibration.

quotes.quote: The members of the first group -- Cygnus A, Casseopeia A, Taurus A, and Virgo A -- all have angular extents of arcminutes and high spectral flux densities, typically in excess of 1000 Jy at = 1m. They are sufficiently strong that their flux densities can be accurately measured with low-resolution telescopes with known gains, but their large scale structure makes them unsuitable for calibration by high-resolution interferometric arrays.
Although Virgo A contains significant compact structure, its angular size of 16 arcminutes is much to large to be useful for calibration purposes for most interferometers.

sources.summary: The simulation paper uses observations of M87 to motivate the external-medium density profile and jet collimation, acceleration, and Bondi-radius scale adopted for AGN-jet models.

quotes.quote: Recent X-ray observations with Chandra inferred a shallow profile ^ 1 , potentially accompanied by a jump, in the external medium profile of the M87 galaxy, near the edge of the central black hole's sphere of influence, or the Bondi radius, r_ B 0.1 kpc russelletal2015 .

For instance, the M87 jet collimates into a parabola (e.g., nakamuraandasada2013 and references therein) and accelerates to Lorentz factors 6 (e.g., birettaetal99, meyeretal2013 ) over 6 orders of magnitude distance before it reaches r_ B .
The X-ray observations of M87 russelletal2015 suggest that a change in the density profile occurs within a factor of a few of r_ B , with the asymptotic density slope 1 .

sources.summary: The paper applies kink-instability results to M87, arguing that its jet becomes nonlinear near where acceleration stops and may transition from Poynting-dominated to kinetic-energy-dominated flow.

quotes.quote: As a tentative application, we use our results to interpret the dynamics of the jet in the nearby active galaxy M87. We show that the kink instability becomes non-linear at a distance from the central black hole comparable to where the jet stops accelerating. Hence (at least for this object), the kink instability of the jet is a good candidate to drive the transition from a Poynting-dominated to a kinetic-energy-dominated flow.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as the principal VLBI test case for GRMHD jet predictions, linking its supermassive black hole and parsec-scale synchrotron jet to non-thermal electron injection.

quotes.quote: Future millimeter/sub-millimeter (mm/sub-mm) very-long-baseline interferometric (VLBI) observations of supermassive black hole candidates, such as the one at the center of M87, can verify this GRMHD jet model and its associated non-thermal electron injection mechanism.

With forthcoming mm/sub-mm VLBI observations of AGN jets, in particular the one in M87 by the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) doe12,lu14,kin15,ric15 and the Greenland Telescope (GLT) nak13a,ino14,raf16 , definitive observable emission signatures like those presented in this work will prove essential in testing theoretical models.

Observations of non-thermal synchrotron emission of the M87 jet beyond pc scales e.g., per99,per01,had11,hom16,mer16 indicate that subsequent re-heating (injecting energy into electrons) far from the emission region discussed here is necessary.

sources.summary: The paper describes a 2017 multiwavelength M 87 campaign coordinated with the Event Horizon Telescope, finding its VERITAS flux consistent with a low state after 25 hours.

quotes.quote: Another recent highlight is our participation in an international MWL campaign on M 87 in March-April 2017. Here we coordinated our observations with the new Event Horizon Telescope to test if any high-energy flickering in the low-emission state is related with any new phenomena near the core of M 87. The VHE flux during our 25 h of observations was consistent with the low state and our total exposure on M 87 is now 290 h.

sources.summary: The simulation paper uses M87 as an observational analogue, citing its X-ray loops and cavities, VLA jet bend, and X-ray cavity alignments around radio-emitting plasma.

quotes.quote: The deep X-ray image of the M87 reveals a series of loops and cavities that are thought to have been produced by a series of outbursts by a swivelling jet.
The bend resembles the prominent feature seen in the VLA image of M87 http://images.nrao.edu/57 by owen_M87radio_2000 .

When producing synthetic soft X-ray images of the cluster, run 2030 shows alignment of several cavities filled with radio-emitting plasma, similar to what is observed in the X-ray gas around the jet of M87.

sources.summary: The paper uses VCC 1316 as the largest globular-cluster sample, identifies it as a representative Type 1a CDF system, and notes ACS coverage reaches only its inner region.

quotes.quote: Figure fig2 shows an example of the best model CDF, the observed CDF, and the K-S statistics for the case of VCC 1316 (M87) that has the largest GC sample.
Most luminous galaxies ( M_ B 19 ) favor this type, and VCC 1316 (M87) and VCC 1226 (M49) show the representative GC CDFs of this type.
For instance, ACS WFC covers only the inner region ( 1 R_ eff ) of VCC 1316 (M87), the largest galaxy in our sample.

sources.summary: The review uses M87 as a foundational AGN jet example, citing VLBI evidence for spine-sheath velocity structure and limb brightening down to a few gravitational radii.

quotes.quote: The 43 GHz VLBI observations of M87 mertens16 show clearly a mildly relativistic velocity component along the sheath and a faster component =2.5 along the spine. The global mm VLBI network has also been used at 86 GHz to study the jet of M87 down to 7 r_ g kim18a and shows limb brightening, and hence evidence of a spine-sheath structure down to this scale, which therefore appears to be anchored to the inner portion of the accretion disk. The result of stacked images from this study is shown in Figure fig:m87mon .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87-like parameters to model stochastic SMBH motion and argues stellar encounters can produce a parsec-scale off-center displacement compatible with M87.

quotes.quote: For the galactic case, we find that the SMBH experiences a Brownian-like motion with a typical displacement from the geometric center of the Galaxy of a few parsecs, for system parameters compatible with M87. stellar dynamics, black hole physics, methods: n-body simulations, methods: statistical.

Once established the convergence between our model and the direct collisional N- body dynamics we have investigated the possibility that certain SMBHs that appear to be off-centered in the parent galaxy [ see e.g. the case of M87; ; but see also ] are effectively diffusing due to several dynamical encounters with stars. In Figure fig2 , we show the evolution of the radial coordinate measured from the centre of the parent model (i.e. the centre of mass of the galaxy) for a M_ BH =6 10^9M_ SMBH starting at rest in a galaxy with stellar and dark matter density distributions (2.1), total mass M_ gal =3 10^ 12 M_ , = 1.2 (for both stellar and dark components), r_c=3 kpc, and a dark to visible matter ratio of 6 , [ parameters roughly corresponding to the case of M87, e.g. , ] .

sources.summary: The paper models M87's EHT-scale black hole and jet emission, noting its LLAGN SMBH, famous extended jets, and a fiducial GRMHD model matching the observed crescent ring and jet non-detection.

quotes.quote: The EHT has captured the first horizon-scale images of the central SMBH in the low luminosity AGN (LLAGN; ) M87 at 230 GHz ( ).

It is interesting and also surprising, in a sense, to note in this context that no clear jet images were obtained in the EHT observation of M87, despite the fact that M87 is famous in exhibiting extended jet features in optical and in longer wavelength radio bands.

Our fiducial model with the black hole spin of a_ BH =0.9M_ BH , can reproduce the asymmetric crescent-shape photon ring and absence of jet features as are observed in the recent EHT observations of M87.

sources.summary: The review highlights M87 (Virgo A) as a benchmark AGN jet: VLBI resolves edge-brightened horizon-scale structure, the jet shows variability, and Event Horizon Telescope imaging probes its central black-hole environment.

quotes.quote: Very long baseline interferometry has allowed the jet of M87 (Virgo A) to be imaged in the radio on scales as small as 10 gravitational radii asada_structure_2012,hada_high-sensitivity_2016,walker_structure_2018 , revealing an edge-brightened morphology with an apparent transition from parabolic to conical streamlines.

However, short-timescale changes can also occur in AGN, examples being blazar flares aharonian_exceptional_2007 , jet variability in e.g. M87 giannios_fast_2010 and Pictor A marshall_flare_2010 , and even variability in radio galaxy hotspots hardcastle_chandra_2005 .

The Event Horizon Telescope has recently obtained stunning images of the horizon-scale environment around the central black hole in M87 eht_first_2019 , an AGN that possesses one of the best studied, and first discovered curtis1918 astrophysical jets.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 to test MAD hadronic emission: proton synchrotron explains Fermi data, Bethe-Heitler pairs reproduce quiescent TeV emission, and MeV emission should be detectable by future satellites.

quotes.quote: For M87, the proton synchrotron emission accounts for the Fermi data, and the secondary pairs by Bethe-Heitler process reproduce the TeV gamma-rays for the quiescent state detected by MAGIC. TeV gamma-rays from M87 are strongly variable, and the flaring activities should be attributed to jets as they correlate with the radio and/or X-ray flares hess06m87,2012ApJ...746..151A,2014ApJ...788..165H . The primary electrons and pairs by pair production emit MeV gamma-rays, which are detectable by proposed MeV satellites, such as AMEGO 2017ICRC...35..798M , e-ASTROGAM 2017ExA....44...25D and GRAMS 2020APh...114..107A .

sources.summary: The paper uses the EHT imaging of M87 as motivation for space VLBI, emphasizing its 40 as SMBH shadow and 1.3 mm, Earth-baseline-limited image.

quotes.quote: Recently, the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT), a mm-wave Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) array, has observed the 40 as diameter shadow of the SMBH in M87 at 1.3 mm, showing that the emitting region is smaller than the black-hole shadow.
Further impetus to a new space VLBI mission has been recently provided by the successful imaging of the shadow of M87 by Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) .
The first EHT image of M87 a wavelength of 1.3 mm (230 GHz) and had a resolution of 20 as, close to the fringe spacing of a 1 Earth diameter baseline at that wavelength.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 87 SMBH mass and spin to derive light-boson constraints, adopting a Salpeter-timescale black-hole lifetime that weakens the constraint relative to prior work.

quotes.quote: In Fig. scalar_vector , we also show the constraints from M 87, with M_ bh =(6.5 0.7) 10^ 9 M_ and a_ * =0.9 0.1 . Here we take _ bh 4.5 10^ 7 years rather than 10^ 9 years as in Ref. . This is why our constraint is not as strong as that in Ref. for this specific object.

sources.summary: The review cites M87 as a rare spatially resolved jet case, discusses evidence that its inner core is pair-plasma dominated, and notes knots within its collimated jet.

quotes.quote: With the exception of recent observations of jet of M87 by the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) Jimenez+2018 , there is a dearth of spatially resolved jet observations on scales below R 50R_ in for any system.
Combining radio spectral data, predictions from synchrotron-self absorption, and kinematics argued that M87 is likely pair plasma in the core on scales below 0.01pc.
Some like M87 exhibit knots or bright spots within their collimated structure.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a synchrotron-emission application, comparing measured flux from an extended region and the quiet core with spectra predicted by relativistic particle distributions.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of the radio-sources As a first example we analyze the spectrum of an extended region around M87, see as example Figure 1 in where the flux in Jy as function of the frequency is reported in the range 9 10^9 Hz < < 2 10^ 18 Hz . Figure radiombrel reports the measured and theoretical flux in the range 9 10^9 Hz < < 2 10^ 12 Hz for the quiet core of M87.

Two astrophysical applications are given: the spectral distribution of emission in the core of M87 in the framework of the synchrotron emissivity and an explanation for the steep spectra sources in the framework of the synchrotron emissivity for the relativistic MJ distribution.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a giant elliptical AGN with a long-studied extragalactic jet, modeling polarized emission and positron effects near its central black hole.

quotes.quote: The central supermassive black hole in the giant elliptical galaxy M87 launches an extragalactic jet which extends from several to tens of kiloparsecs across the EM spectrum. M87's jet has been studied for over a century from radio to ray wavelengths e.g. Curtis1918,Palmer67,Nagar2001,Doeleman2012,Whysong2004,Madrid2009,DiMatteo2003,Aharonian2006,MWL2021 . Very-Long-Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) images at radio and sub-millimeter wavelengths show a dynamical, polarized, limb-brightened forward jet and counter jet in the central parsecs of M87 e.g. Junor1999,Asada2012,Hada2016,Walker2018,Kim2018,Kravchenko2020 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4486/M87 as a giant elliptical in Virgo, detecting it at high gamma-ray significance with a power-law spectrum and fitting its multi-wavelength SED with a power-law electron distribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486 (M87) M87 is a giant elliptical galaxy located in Virgo cluster at a redshift, z=0.00428 ( cap11 ).

NGC 4486: The source was detected with high statistical significance TS = 1844.52 ( 43 ) with our likelihood analysis.The average spectrum is well-defined by a power law of photon index, = 2.04 0.03 and integrated flux, F _ 0.1-300 GeV = 1.94 ( 0.09) 10 ^ 11 ergs cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

For NGC 4486, we find the best-fit describing the SED is a power-law which is consistent with the best-fit obtained for the combined and MAGIC data ( magic ) for 2012-2015, hence we have used a power-law electron distribution in our modeling to fit the multi-wavelength SED.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC 4486, Messier 87, as the Virgo central dominant galaxy with a bright 3C 274 core, jet, cavity-related lobe, and extended tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486 NGC 4486 or Messier 87, is the central dominant galaxy of the Virgo cluster. Here, we summarize the results obtained from the archival A, B, and C configuration data ( Figure fig:chandra_vla_c a ). \ The bright radio core, 3C 274, was detected along with its innermost radio jet, which is coincident with the X-ray emission of the jet marshall2002 .

sources.summary: The paper adds M87 as a radio galaxy within an IceCube track region, noting its blazar-like jet orientation, superluminal motion, and HBL-like high-energy emission and variability.

quotes.quote: To these, we further add M87 The radio galaxy M87, one of the brightest objects in the extragalactic sky, is well within the uncertainty region of the neutrino track IceCube-141126A (G20: see also Section sec:Introduction ). As pointed out by G20 its jet inclination and superluminal motion make it almost a blazar. Moreover, M87 is HBL-like due its strong GeV and TeV emission combined with a flat ray spectrum and large flux variability, although the complexity of its radio, optical, and near-infrared emission prevent us from reliably estimating .

sources.summary: The paper describes M87 as a giant radio galaxy with HBL-like properties, possible IceCube-141126A association, and reported flare-neutrino excess.

quotes.quote: A recent investigation aimed at the detection of flare neutrino emission in the 10 year IceCube data set reported a time-dependent neutrino excess in the northern hemisphere at the level of 3 associated with four sources: M87, a giant radio galaxy, TXS 0506+056 and GB6 J1542+6129, two IBL blazars, and NGC 1068.
M87, which has properties similar to those of HBL objects, is also the possible counterpart of the IceCube-141126A track-like event.

A very recent search for neutrino flare emission in the IceCube 10 year data set Abbasi2021 reported possible flares at the level of 3 from NGC 1068, two IHBL blazars TXS 0506+056, GB6 J1542+6129, and M87, a giant radio galaxy with properties similar to that of IHBL objects that was already noticed as a possible counterpart of the track IceCube-141126A Giommidissecting .

sources.summary: M 87 is described as an early-detected nearby radio galaxy, later found to emit X-rays and detected above TeV energies by Whipple-era telescopes.

quotes.quote: The development of the radar technique during World War II enabled in the 1950s the first detections of radio galaxies such as Centaurus A (at a luminosity distance d 4 Mpc), M 87 ( d 16 Mpc) and Cygnus A ( d 230 Mpc).
The late 1960s and early 1970s revealed that some of these radio galaxies and quasars, such as M 87, Centaurus A and 3C 273, also emit X rays.
Seven other blazars and a single radio galaxy, M 87, had been detected by 2005 with the Whipple telescope and its successors such as HEGRA (La Palma, Canary Islands) and CAT (Pyrenees, France) .

sources.summary: The paper handles NGC 4486/M87 specially because its bright central AGN and jet cause severe Chandra pile-up, requiring archival short-frame observations and tailored spectral extraction.

quotes.quote: In the case of the galaxy NGC 4486 (M87), the bright central AGN and jet emission leads to very strong pile-up effects within the central few arcsec of Chandra ACIS-S observations obtained in the classical full-array 3.2 s exptime mode. Following the analysis of , and , we also utilized archival observations in the 1/8th subarray 0.4 s exptime mode. The spectral files for the short frame-time observations were extracted from circular annuli up to 30 arcsec from the centre, whereas for observations in the full-frame 3.2 s exptime mode the spectra were extracted in the 15-230 arcsec range.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as the benchmark SMBH-shadow host, emphasizing conflicting stellar- and gas-dynamical mass estimates and EHT shadow-based mass agreement with stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: As an example, M87^ has two mass determinations to date: stellar kinematics gives M_ BH =(6.6 0.4) 10^9 M _ while gas dynamics gives M_ BH =(3.5^ +0.9 _ 0.7 ) 10^9 M _ . The two determinations have an accuracy of 7 and 22 respectively, but they exhibit a 3.5 tension showing that systematic uncertainties are still far from being under control. The EHT collaboration has also determined the mass from the shadow size of M87 to be M_ BH = 6.5 0.2|_ stat 0.7|_ sys 10^9 M _ showing good agreement with the measurement of and a 3 discrepancy with that of .

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 and its well-studied globular cluster system to train classifiers, relying on its pre-classified catalogues and distance.

quotes.quote: In this study, we take advantage of M87's well-studied GC system to implement supervised machine learning (ML) classification algorithms --- specifically random forest and neural networks --- to identify GCs from foreground stars and background galaxies using ground-based photometry from the Canada-France-Hawai'i Telescope (CFHT).
We train our models on sources from within M87, as it is one of the most thoroughly studied galaxies besides our own, which provides us with large catalogues of pre-classified data.

Once loaded into the ML model programs, columns were added to each dataset indicating four colours ( u-g, g-, g-i, and g-z ) and all magnitudes were converted to absolute magnitudes assuming a distance of 16.5 Mpc, the distance to M87 Strader2011 , for all sources (Fig. f:mags ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies M87 as Virgo's massive central elliptical hosting an AGN, observed for legacy value, whose turbulent high-dispersion spectrum fits better with wider masks.

quotes.quote: The third is Virgo's massive central elliptical, M87, hosting an AGN Lea1982 . Our catalogue selection generally avoided galaxies with active centres, but we observed M87 for its legacy value. A visual inspection of the fitting of M87's spectrum shows a better fit when the wider masks are used due to the system's high velocity dispersion and turbulent central region.

sources.summary: The paper treats Vir A as a nearby powerful AGN whose hotspot absence and simulated flux depend strongly on extragalactic magnetic-field deflections.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxies, Centaurus A (Cen A, NGC 5128), Virgo A (Vir A, M87), and Fornax A (For A, NGC 1316) are the closest and most powerful nearby AGNs van2012radio,cavagnolo2010 .
The absence of hotspots in the Vir A direction can be explained by the effect of the extragalactic magnetic fields in the propagation of the particles Dolag_2009,de_Oliveira_2022 .
The Prim2R EGMF model suppresses the energy flux of NGC 5055, NGC 3628, NGC 3627, NGC 4631, M51, NGC 3556, NGC 3079, and Vir A.

sources.summary: The paper says VERITAS observed M87 during the 2017 EHT multiwavelength campaign, when the source was historically faint but still produced a 3.8-sigma analysis result.

quotes.quote: During the 2017 Event Horizon Telescope MWL campaign on M87, VERITAS captured the source in its historically low state, but still dominating over the nearest knot, HST-1 . The most complete simultaneous MWL spectrum was reported in that study, which included 15 h of quality-selected VERITAS data. The analysis resulted in an overall statistical significance of 3.8 .

sources.summary: The paper cites VLBA observations of the M 87 jet showing decomposable proper-motion substructures, supporting a picture of AGN jets made of many small blobs.

quotes.quote: Besides, applied the wavelet-based image segmentation and evaluation analysis to the jet image of M 87 obtained from 43 GHz Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations. Using the segmented wavelet decomposition (SWD) and intermediate wavelet decomposition (IWD) methods, the VLBA image of M 87 jet can be decomposed into numerous local regions of different proper motions, implying the existence of discrete substructures inside the jet.
According to the VLBA observations of the M 87 jet 2016A&A...595A..54M and MHD simulations e.g., 2009MNRAS.395.2183Y , jets of AGNs could consist of many small blobs on different scales.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as an early spectroscopic case where central velocity dispersion implied an excessive mass-to-light ratio and a compact massive central object.

quotes.quote: One of the pioneering works on this line was which studied the kinematics of the galaxy M87 by using spectroscopic data. The analysis showed a sharp increase in the velocity dispersion in the inner parts of the galaxy ( 110 ), inconsistent with the expected mass-to-light ratio. To explain such discrepancy, the authors hypothesised the existence of a compact massive object with 5 10^9 at the center of M87.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a massive local-galaxy benchmark, arguing its stellar mass must have grown greatly without proportionate black-hole growth, favoring continued star formation over mergers.

quotes.quote: For example, M87 is estimated to have a stellar mass of M_* = 10^ 12.3 and a black hole mass of M_ BH = 10^ 9.8 Gebhardt2009,Akiyama2019 .
Thus, the stellar mass of M87 must have grown significantly since its apparent turnoff.

An alternative mechanism in which M87 continued to form stars efficiently after its apparent turnoff, without merging and without growing its central supermassive black hole, would provide another solution.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4486 as a slow rotator with relatively large velocity errors but usable data from 20 to 60 where the rotation curve flattens.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486. This galaxy's slow rotation makes the errors relatively large in the velocity measurements, but outside of 20 the data is statistically consistent with a flattening of the rotation curve out to the last datapoint at 60 . The data between these radii are therefore used.

sources.summary: The review describes M87 as an elliptical galaxy with the first observed jet, whose inner one-sided structure is attributed to Doppler beaming and gives the best current jet resolution.

quotes.quote: Observations The first jet observed was that in the nucleus of the elliptical galaxy M87 in the Virgo cluster. The modern representation is given in Fig. ( ) (Perlman 1999). In this case, the jet emerges from no more than 100 times the gravitational radius of the central hole ( M=3 10^9 M _ ; GM/^2= 4 10^ 14 cm) and propagates radially outward for a distance 3 10^ 22 cm, seven orders of magnitude larger. The jet, however, is neither homogeneous nor smooth. Superimposed upon an overall decrease in surface brightness as the jets expand away from the central hole are strong side to side variations and bright features. These may reflect a time-dependence at the jet origin or independent, local instabilities. At small radii, the M87 jet is strongly one-sided, and this is generally attributed to Doppler beaming and adduced as evidence for relativistic outflow.
At present, as we have remarked, the best resolution has been achieved in M87 ( 100m ).

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as a low-luminosity elliptical-galaxy AGN example, noting its low luminosity, negligible disk irradiation, and spectra consistent with wind and no-wind truncation models.

quotes.quote: The low luminosity in the elliptical galaxy M87 (NGC 4486) was pointed out by Reynolds et al. (1996).
Direct and indirect (by scattering on the clouds) irradiation is negligible for the example M 87 because of the low luminosity.
For M 87 both, models with and without wind loss agree with the observed spectra.

sources.summary: The paper uses gamma-ray and radio limits to constrain cosmic-ray proton injection by M87, comparing inferred luminosities with published jet-power estimates.

quotes.quote: Assuming a plausible value for the CRp diffusion coefficient , we find the central CRp injection luminosity of M 87 to be limited to 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 /(10^ 29 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) .

The limits which are strongest in the case of M87 in the Virgo cluster represent conservative bounds since we choose the active CRp diffusion scenario resulting in spectral steepening of the CRp population. We obtain even tighter limits when assuming a passive advective transport of the CRp in a turbulent flow in which case we infer L_ = L( _ ) 10^ 42 h_ 70 ^ 1/2 erg s ^ 1 _0 10^ 29 cm^2 s^ 1 with L( _ )= 4.5, 4.8, 7.2, and 20.9 for _ = 2.4, 2.5, 2.7, and 2.9, respectively. These values are slightly smaller than instantaneous jet power estimates of M87 being of the order of L_ jet 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 1996ApJ...467..597B,2002ApJ...579..560Y . In general, this demonstrates the ability of future high resolution ray observations to constrain the energy fraction of CRp escaping from the radio plasma.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates a previous UHECR source hypothesis for M87, describing an apparent alignment in a halo-field model and arguing it is likely a focusing coincidence.

quotes.quote: If such field is introduced, the positions of 11 out of 13 EHECRs from Haverah Park, Volcano Ranch, Fly's Eye and AGASA should fall within 20 ^ spherical cap around M87 position.
They proved that this at the face-value exciting fact, that M87 could be a single source of UHECRs, is simply based on the fundamental property of the used magnetic field model in halo.

The used model of an azimuthal field is simply focusing all positions into the direction of Galactic north pole and M87 is lying near to this pole, and so the small angular distance between computed EHECR positions and between M87 is probably just an interesting coincidence without fundamental physical importance.

sources.summary: The review uses M87 to argue against shock-dominated LINER excitation: the disk-based shock claim avoids the nucleus, while the nuclear UV-optical spectrum fits photoionization.

quotes.quote: Dopita (1997) used the spectrum of the circumnuclear disk of M87 to advance the view that LINERs are shock excited. This argument is misleading because their analysis deliberately avoids the nucleus. Sabra et al. (2003) demonstrate that the UV--optical spectrum of the nucleus \ of M87 is best explained by a multi-component photoionization model.

sources.summary: The paper treats M 87 as a relatively powerful FR I radio galaxy whose infrared observations show no significant mid-infrared nuclear excess, supporting absence of a hidden quasar-like accretion process.

quotes.quote: A fine test for such a scenario has been obtained in the case of M 87, a famous and relatively powerful FR I radio galaxy . M 87 was observed in the infrared at 10 m with the Keck and Gemini telescope, and no significant mid--IR nuclear excess is found. This supports the absence of a hidden quasar-like accretion process in the nucleus, which would heat the surrounding obscuring dust, thus producing a strong thermal IR excess, when compared to the optical observed flux.

sources.summary: The review cites M87 as showing subsonic Bondi-Hoyle-Lyttleton flow, while higher-resolution work found that its active nucleus drives an outflow, complicating the simple accretion picture.

quotes.quote: 1980ApJ...242..511D observed M87, and found evidence for subsonic Bondi--Hoyle--Lyttleton flow. However, higher resolution observations by 2000ApJ...543..611O suggest that this simple picture is not sufficient. In particular, the active nucleus of M87 drives an outflow.

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a nearby powerful AGN example for UHECR acceleration, placing it in Virgo at 18 Mpc and modeling magnetic-field propagation.

quotes.quote: Possible cosmologically nearby objects include Cen A (distance of 5 Mpc) and M87 in the Virgo cluster (distance of 18 Mpc).
Figure cipanp shows an example for protons accelerated at the powerful AGN M87 in case is connected with our galaxy by 3 Mpc wide cylinder of 10 nG magnetic field. The distance to M87 is 18 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as an integral-field dynamical example, emphasizing that this giant elliptical has a resolved black-hole sphere of influence, a large core, and well-sampled nuclear stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: To demonstrate what IF observations can provide, we present here the recovery of the orbital distribution from general axisymmetric three-integral dynamical modeling of the IF stellar kinematics of M87, which is part of the \ survey (Bacon et al. \ 2001b; de Zeeuw et al. \ 2002). This galaxy is an ideal candidate for this ground-based study as its BH sphere of influence R_ BH 1.4'' is resolved even in natural seeing. Moreover, the core, whose structure is thought to have been shaped by the BH binary, has a radius R_c 7.5'' (Faber et al. \ 1997) and is very well sampled by the observed \ kinematics.

sources.summary: The Chandra overview uses M87 as an active-galaxy jet example, noting multiwavelength structure and X-ray-bright knots near the central core.

quotes.quote: Figure  f:m87 \ of M87 illustrates multiwavelength observations of the jets from active galaxies. The \ X-ray image (Marshall et al. 2002) shows an irregular, knotty structure similar to that seen at radio and optical (Perlman et al. 2001) wavelengths. However, the knots near the central core are much brighter in X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as an example of central ICM enrichment, arguing stellar mass loss can replenish its inner gas and iron budget while its abundance ratios require comparatively high silicon yields.

quotes.quote: Consideration of the budget of the central ICM for example for the case of M87 shows that the inner 10 kpc region of the ICM, roughly the region of the scale radius of cD galaxies (or approximately half light radius), contains a gas mass of about 2 10^9 M _ and an iron mass of about 6 10^6 M _ . Both can be replenished by stellar mass loss from M87 in about 2 - 3 Gyrs if we adopt a stellar mass loss rate of 2.5 10^ 11 L_B (Ciotti et al. 1991) and a Fe production with a supernova rate of 0.15 SNU (Cappelaro et al. 1999).
The data points for M87 are the most extreme in requiring a relatively large Si yield (Matsushita et al. 2003).

sources.summary: The paper shows NGC 4486's ionized-gas velocity field, where gas spirals inward and a rapidly rotating central component is linked to the black hole's Keplerian disk.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:sau_oas_comp f shows the gas velocity field for NGC 4486 (M87). shows a stream of gas spiraling to the center of the galaxy, connecting smoothly to a component rotating around the center. The observations show that this central component rotates much more rapidly than the surrounding gas, and in fact is probably related to the Keplerian disk of material orbiting the central super-massive black hole ford94 .

sources.summary: The paper cites M 87 as the first photographed extragalactic jet and describes the later recognition of its blue, polarized synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: Curtis photographed the jet in M 87 from the Lick Observatory in 1918. While it was soon found to be blue, the emission process was not understood despite Schott's detailed calculations of synchrotron radiation in his 1912 book. Finally after Shklovskii introduced this emission mechanism into astrophysics Baade (1956) showed the jet to be highly polarized which clinched it.

sources.summary: The paper cites M87 as a cluster-dominant elliptical with an extended globular-cluster system and a massive dark-matter halo core inferred from gas, planetary-nebula, and globular-cluster kinematics.

quotes.quote: Very extended GC systems are found in cluster-dominant ellipticals (such as M87, M49, and NGC 1399) , which is consistent with them being agents rather than victims of stripping.

In M49 and M87, massive DM halo cores are found from constant-to-rising profiles of gas temperature, and of velocity dispersion profiles of PNe and GCs . Different orbital properties are implied between the PNe and GCs, which should constrain central galaxy evolution scenarios.

sources.summary: The paper uses 86 GHz global VLBI imaging of M87 to resolve its inner jet on scales of tens of Schwarzschild radii, constraining jet formation near the SMBH.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show in a new global-VLBI image of the inner jet of M87 at 86 GHz (Figure 1). At a distance of 18.7 Mpc, the angular resolution of 300 x 60 as corresponds to a spatial scale of 30 x 6 light days, or 100 x 20 Schwarzschild-radii (assuming 3 x 10^6 M _ for the SMBH). The existence of a fully developed jet on such small spatial scales gives important new constraints for the theory of jet formation and may even indicate rotation of the central SMBH (via comparison with the width of the light cylinder).

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 to demonstrate 86 GHz global VLBI imaging, resolving the inner jet on scales of tens of light-days and constraining jet formation near the central SMBH.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show in Fig. m87 a new global-VLBI image of the inner jet of M87 at 86 GHz (taken from Krichbaum et al. ). At a distance of 18.7 Mpc, the angular resolution of 300 60 as corresponds to a spatial scale of 30 6 light days, or 100 20 Schwarzschild-radii (assuming 3 10^6 M _ for the SMBH). The existence of a fully developed jet on such small spatial scales gives important new constraints for the theory of jet formation and may even indicate rotation of the central SMBH (via comparison with the width of the light cylinder).

sources.summary: The paper treats M87 as a LINER/FRI radio galaxy with a strong compact radio core, variable UV flux, and adopted high-state HST UV measurements.

quotes.quote: M87 (NGC 4486) - A 2 cm VLBA measurement of the unresolved core of M87 by Kellerman et al. (2004), with 1 mas resolution, is 3 Jy, consistent with a VLA ( 1'' resolution) measurement by Biretta et al. (1991). The UV flux is variable on short time-scales (Perlman et al. 2003; M05) as well as on long ones (M05). At 2500 , I will adopt a high flux of 100 10^ 17 , based on an HST/FOC measurement by Maoz et al. (1996).

sources.summary: The paper uses M87 as an active-galaxy benchmark whose luminosity, HST photometry, and gas-kinematic black-hole mass test correlations between black holes and spheroids.

quotes.quote: Observations appear to confirm this; e.g., the (active) galaxies M87 and NGC 1068 have similar luminosities, but is 10^ 2.5 times larger in M87 (cf. Figure 1a below).

Nonetheless, simple models of adiabatic BH growth for observed photometry of M87 (Young 1978; Lauer 1992; Crane 1993) and several other galaxies imply BH masses that agree well with kinematic determinations. I have therefore started a study of adiabatic BH growth models for a large sample of galaxies with published HST photometry. These models may be particularly relevant for core galaxies, for which the observed break in the brightness profile may be associated with an originally homogeneous core. I have used the software of Q95 to fit the photometric models of Y80 to all core galaxies in the sample of F97 with M_B < 20 (van der Marel 1998, in preparation). The models fit well in the central few arcsec (RMS residual 0.05 mag/arcsec ^2 ), and the photometrically inferred appear meaningful: the kinematically determined for M87 and NGC 3379 (see Figure 1a) are reproduced to within 0.12 and 0.50 dex, respectively.

sources.summary: The article presents M87 as a giant elliptical with a long history as a supermassive black-hole candidate, supported by HST gas-disc spectroscopy and its jet geometry.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of the giant elliptical M87 in the nearby Virgo cluster has also a long story as a supermassive black hole candidate. Several independent observations are consistent with a 1-3 10^ 9 M_ black hole accreting in a slow, inefficient mode. A disc of gas is orbiting in a plane perpendicular to a spectacular jet; recent spectroscopic observations of the Hubble Space Telescope show redshifted and blueshifted components of the disc, which can be interpreted by Doppler effect as parts of the disc on each side of the hole are receding and approaching from us.

sources.summary: For 3C 274/M87, the paper adopts previous evidence for X-ray core variability and uses the June 1995 core count rate, finding consistency with prior results.

quotes.quote: 3C 274 (M87) Harris, Biretta Junor (1997) present evidence that the X-ray core of M87 varies by 20 per cent on time-scales of years. We use their core count rate for June 1995. The total counts tabulated are for the source region they define, a 276-arcsec radius circle with background taken from a 280--300 arcsec annulus, both centred around the X-ray core, and our results are consistent with theirs.
